races.race_name: Sky Bet ebfstallions.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Persian Spring
runners.spotlight: Late April foal by Persian Force; shaped with some encouragement when 5l fifth of seven on his 5f debut at the recent Newmarket Guineas meeting; should improve for last year's winning stable.
runners.comment: Some encouragement when fifth of seven on 5f Newmarket debut; likely improver

runners.name: Rlasthope
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when fifth of 15 in the Brocklesby at Doncaster and duly improved when pushing recent Lily Agnes winner Adonius close at Musselburgh (also 5f, good to soft) a fortnight later, albeit he received 5lb from that Rebecca Menzies-trained gelding; likely to stay 6f.
runners.comment: Promise both starts, latterly pushing a useful sort close; 6f should be within range

runners.name: Sir Sirius
runners.spotlight: Fairly late February foal; 350,000gns yearling by Starman; half-brother to four winners, including Mehmento (7f including AW/Listed; RPR 109) and Immortal Beauty (6f turf/7f AW 2yo; 91); stable's 2yos going well, including a couple of promising first-time-out scorers; market confidence would look significant; yard also runs Statten Island.
runners.comment: 350,000gns Starman colt; one of two for Kevin Ryan, whose 2yos have started well
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Statten Island
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros fairly early March foal; 180,000gns yearling by Starspangledbanner; half-brother to three winners, notably Raaeb (5f-7f including 2yo/AW/UAE dirt; RPR 108); one of two interesting debutants from the Kevin Ryan yard and the betting should help to separate them.
runners.comment: One of two interesting debutants from the Kevin Ryan yard; betting helpful

runners.name: Saxy Lass
runners.spotlight: Early February foal; 13,000gns yearling; Saxon Warrior filly out of a 1m2f winner (RPR 71); The Resdev Roman is the more obvious of the yard's pair on paper.
runners.comment: Saxon Warrior filly who would be a surprise winner on debut

runners.name: The Resdev Roman
runners.spotlight: Early March foal who cost £23,000 at last month's Doncaster breeze-up sale; half-sister to winners American Hope (French/German 7.5f/1m including AW; RPR 93), Tryst (8.6f AW 2yo; 86) and Vino Bello (French 1m-1m1f including 2yo/AW; 73); bred to have a future but will need to be very useful to take this first time out.
runners.comment: Bred to have a future but will need to be very useful to take this on debut

runners.name: Clash Of Hearts
runners.spotlight: Early March foal by Mehmas; 120,000gns yearling; half-brother to yard's smart 2024 juvenile Symbol Of Strength (won Group 3 Sirenia and third in Gimcrack; later successful in Hong Kong); would enter calculations if the betting vibes are strong.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a smart 2yo for this yard in 2024; needs a betting check

runners.name: Leave The Bag In
runners.spotlight: Early May foal by Cotai Glory; 62,000gns yearling half-brother to winners Galileo Dame (1m2f Flat, RPR 97; 2m hurdle) and Mauna Queen (Norwegian 7f/1m4f); dam 9.4f winner (78); not necessarily bred to be an early type for yard also responsible for Clash Of Hearts.
runners.comment: Not really bred to be an early type; yard also responsible for Clash Of Hearts

runners.name: Mr Minz
runners.spotlight: Early February foal by Minzaal; 70,000euros yearling who is closely related to 6f 2yo winner French Affair (RPR 78); stable isn't really associated with first-time-out 2yo success.
runners.comment: Best watched for yard not really known for its prowess with early 2yos

runners.name: Rock Steady Beat
runners.spotlight: March foal by Minzaal; 110,000gns yearling; half-brother to five winners, notably Happy Romance (5f/6f including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 112); makes obvious paper appeal for a leading stable which won this with a newcomer in 2024.
runners.comment: Makes obvious paper appeal for a leading stable which won this with a newcomer in 2024

runners.name: Star People
runners.spotlight: January foal by Sioux Nation; £85,000 yearling; second foal of a mare who was placed over 10.5f/1m4f in France (RPR 73); best watched on his debut unless the betting hints otherwise.
runners.comment: Best watched on debut unless the betting suggests otherwise

runners.name: Sue's Last Chance
runners.spotlight: 62,000euros early February foal; 125,000euros yearling by Cotai Glory; brother to 5f AW 2yo winner Elliott (RPR 62); dam US Listed-placed 1m/8.5f winner (90); stable yet to get off the mark with 2yos in 2026.
runners.comment: Newcomer from a yard which has yet to get off mark with 2yos in 2026

runners.name: The Ginger Kid
runners.spotlight: Late March foal by Mehmas; 110,000euros yearling; third foal out of an unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 1 (Cheveley Park) winner Tiggy Wiggy; trainer had a first-time-out 2yo winner at Nottingham on Saturday; would enter calculations if the market vibes are strong.
runners.comment: Yard had a first-time-out 2yo winner last weekend; respected if betting vibes strong

runners.name: Toon Army
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros late March foal; 16,000euros yearling; gelded son of Kodiac who is a brother to 5f AW winner Alhather (RPR 77) and half-brother to 5f winner Billie Be Quick (including 2yo; 93); yard wouldn't really be known for debut success in races like this but did have a ready 2yo winner at Beverley on Tuesday (second time out).
runners.comment: Yard had a ready 2yo winner at Beverley on Tuesday so worth a second look in betting

runners.name: Ranting Duke
runners.spotlight: Early April foal by Zoustar; Irish yard has sent out a 2yo winner in Britain this season but this colt offered only minor promise on his 5f Dundalk debut five weeks ago.
runners.comment: Offered only minor promise on 5f Dundalk debut five weeks ago

runners.name: Craven Force
runners.spotlight: No better than mid-division in two outings over 5f at Beverley; needs to find significant improvement for this extra furlong.
runners.comment: No better than mid-division in two outings over 5f at Beverley; outsider
runners.owner: Craven Surface , Mrs T Burns & Partners

races.race_name: Lindum York Handicap

runners.name: Arklow Lad
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won on stable debut at Chelmsford (5f, AW) in February before good third at Wolverhampton (6f, AW), and he was back on the scoresheet in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) last month; could continue to progress for his new connections; chance.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who is 2-3 for his new connections and is in with a chance

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark in small field at Southwell (5f, AW) in March; ran pretty well on next two starts but down the field at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) latest and others are preferred in a hotter race than he's used to.
runners.comment: Won off this mark in small field on AW in March but others preferred in this hot contest

runners.name: Heavenly Heather
runners.spotlight: 5yo who raced over further until this year, and her three career-best RPRs have come on her three attempts at 5f; kept on well for close fourth of 14 at Musselburgh (5f, soft) last month on a rare outing on turf, and she's an each-way contender if handling the forecast better ground.
runners.comment: Has been doing well this year over 5f, at which she is unexposed; each-way contender

runners.name: Air Force One
runners.spotlight: Beat 18 rivals to win over C&D (good) last July and went close over 5.4f here on his next two starts; regained the winning thread at Beverley (5f, good to soft) four weeks ago and he's only 2lb higher today; respected.
runners.comment: Ran some big races here last season and won at Beverley four weeks ago; respected

runners.name: Ziggy's Triton
runners.spotlight: Back on better ground when he returned to form with a win at Musselburgh (5f, good) last month, taking his strike-rate to 5-22; did well to come out of top from a wide draw and this 4yo could go well up 5lb.
runners.comment: Returned to form to win despite wide draw at Musselburgh and he could go well up 5lb

runners.name: Sturlasson
runners.spotlight: Did well for new yard last autumn, winning three times (5f, AW/good to soft), and he returned to form when second of six at Bath (5f, good) recently; however, he may prove vulnerable in this hotter contest.
runners.comment: Returned to form when 2nd of six at Bath but may prove vulnerable in this hotter contest

runners.name: Lethal Nymph
runners.spotlight: Two wins last autumn and he's better than he's shown at Pontefract and Ascot of late; however, this is tougher and he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Two wins last autumn but he needs to better this spring's form, and this race is tough

runners.name: Redorange
runners.spotlight: Last season's 3yo campaign ended with two below-par runs but he may have been ready for a break; reappears on a competitive mark based on last summer's form in big-field 5f handicaps and Ryan Moore, who has an excellent 33% strike-rate for Clive Cox, is booked; could be involved.
runners.comment: Reappears on a competitive mark and Ryan Moore has excellent 33% strike-rate for Clive Cox

runners.name: Blinky
runners.spotlight: Won two 5f AW handicaps early last year and good fourth of 16 in the 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June, when 3lb higher; however, he's 0-11 since his last win and others arrive with more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Won two 5f AW handicaps early last year but 0-11 since; others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Corolla Point
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced, ex-Godolphin 4yo; won on stable debut at Haydock (5f, good) last September and followed up later that month (5f, soft); reappeared with close third to Air Force One at Beverley (5f, good to soft) four weeks ago and this unexposed sort could still have plenty more to offer.
runners.comment: Won first 2 starts for this yard; close 3rd on return; unexposed; could have more to offer

runners.name: Against The Wind
runners.spotlight: Third of 22 in 3yo handicap over C&D (good to firm) at this meeting 12 months ago and progressive on AW since; soft ground may not have suited at Musselburgh last time and he's had wind surgery since; however, a career best is needed today and he has to prove he's just as good on turf as AW.
runners.comment: Has run well in big field over C&D but it's possible he's superior on AW; has had wind op

runners.name: Hammer The Hammer
runners.spotlight: Second of 24 in the 5f 3yo handicap at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm) last June and he showed good speed before fading into sixth of 19 over 6f here (good) last October, when last seen; wind op since; the drop back in trip could be a positive move on his reappearance; can make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Showed good speed over 6f here last October and the return to 5f could be a positive move

runners.name: Copper Knight
runners.spotlight: Hugely admirable 12yo whose seven York wins include this race in 2019 and 2021, and he built upon last month's reappearance second at Ripon (5f, good) to win at Beverley (5f, good to soft) ten days ago; however, there's a suspicion he may find a big-field Class 2 such as this too hot nowadays.
runners.comment: Prolific York winner; scored at Beverley latest; however, this 12yo may find this too hot

runners.name: Eternal Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Trainer won this last year with subsequent Group 1 winner American Affair; progressive last season and she's 1lb lower than when a close third of 18 over C&D (good) last October; however, well beaten on both starts this season (irregular heartbeat last time) and a major turnaround is needed.
runners.comment: Progressive last season and ran really well over C&D last October; yet to shine this term

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: He's been an improved model this year and won a shade cosily at Beverley (5f, good) last month; his subsequent run at Musselburgh (5f, good) three days later, when fourth, may have come too soon and further progress can't be ruled out, but it's necessary with this step up in grade.
runners.comment: Improved this year; further progress not ruled out, but necessary with step up in grade

runners.name: Luna A Inbhir Nis
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was a highly progressive 3yo last season, making it six wins for the campaign when scoring here (5.4f, good) last July; very close third of 19 over this C&D (good to firm) next time; suited by this course and could give a good account on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Front-runner who was a highly progressive 3yo last season, winning here; could go well

runners.name: Toca Madera
runners.spotlight: His form has gone the wrong way since he was third in this last year (good to firm), behind two subsequent Group-race winners, and he needs a good step forward from his recent stable/seasonal debut at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) where he was seventh; however, he's 8lb lower than 12 months ago and could be just the type that David O'Meara excels with; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Third in this last year off 8lb higher; regressive since but early days with his new yard
runners.owner: L Bond & Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi

runners.name: Jakajaro
runners.spotlight: Snapped a lengthy losing sequence when making virtually all to win at Newmarket (5f, good) last month on his reappearance; he needs to back up that performance but did it comfortably last time and, following a 5lb rise, remains well treated on his historic exploits; a possible.
runners.comment: Comfortable win at Newmarket on reappearance; remains well treated on historic exploits

runners.name: Brazen Bolt
runners.spotlight: Won big-field handicaps over C&D (good to firm) and at Goodwood (5f, good) last July, and this 9yo returned from wind surgery with a back-to-form, neck second at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; Harry Vigors removes a valuable 7lb and this prominent-racer could be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last July and back on song when going close on return; could be bang there

runners.name: Stormy Impact
runners.spotlight: Won the big-field 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June and landed another quality handicap at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in July; she lacks a recent run and there is a suspicion that 6f is her optimum trip, but she'll get a strong pace to aim at today and is not ruled out off a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Two wins last season, including the 3yo Dash at Epsom; the strong pace will help today

runners.name: Bergerac
runners.spotlight: 8yo who was inconsistent last season and lacks a recent run; however, he's just 1lb higher than when both second of 13 over C&D (good) last May and winning at Hamilton (5f, good to soft) next time; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: 8yo who was inconsistent last season but is effective here and is not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Squealer
runners.spotlight: Won off 7lb higher over C&D (good) last June and he's one to watch in the betting; an underwhelming autumn was followed by a fairly encouraging reappearance run at Beverley last month, but he was down the field at Thirsk last time and is back to having something to prove.
runners.comment: Won off 7lb higher over C&D last June but his form has gone the wrong way

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Hambleton Handicap

runners.name: Sea Force
runners.spotlight: Won at Yarmouth (1m1f, soft) last September on final 3yo start and returned to form when third to Mirsky in the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago in reapplied cheekpieces (retained), which he may well have won granted a clear run; lightly raced and in top hands; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: May well have won the Thirsk Hunt Cup granted a clear run and he's just 1lb higher today

runners.name: First Principle
runners.spotlight: Having been gelded last autumn he enjoyed a good stint on AW, winning twice (1m/8.6f); soundly beaten at Newcastle (1m, AW) last time and he needs to transfer his improvement back to turf, but he remains lightly raced for his top yard and is not discounted under Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: AW improvement stalled last time but he remains lightly raced and is not discounted

runners.name: Walsingham
runners.spotlight: Ran just four times last year, winning on three occasions (1m, good/soft), and back on the scoresheet at Pontefract (1m, good) a fortnight ago on his second start of the season; did it cosily last time and a 4lb rise may not stop this thriving 6yo.
runners.comment: Did it cosily at Pontefract last time and 4lb rise may not stop this thriving 6yo

runners.name: Shout
runners.spotlight: Progressive 3yo last season (on various ground), winning a big-field 1m handicap at Ascot in September; reappeared with encouraging run in the Lincoln at Doncaster and he's not written off, but he was somewhat underwhelming in the Spring Cup at Newbury last time when favourite.
runners.comment: Progressive 3yo last season; not written off but underwhelming in the Spring Cup last time

runners.name: Cerulean Bay
runners.spotlight: Progressive last term, winning two in a row at Goodwood (1m, good to soft/good) in the autumn, and kept on well for a close fourth on last month's reappearance at Haydock (7f, good to firm); solid claims back up to 1m.
runners.comment: Progressive last term and kept on well for close 4th over 7f on reappearance; solid claims

runners.name: Old Cock
runners.spotlight: Won this off 1lb lower last year (good to firm); however, soundly beaten over C&D (good to firm) last August on final run for Ed Bethell and down the field on last month's stable/seasonal debut at Newbury (1m, good).
runners.comment: Won this off 1lb lower last year but arrives with something to prove this time around

runners.name: Eldrickjones
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last year's best form (including on turf) and ran okay when third of six at Southwell (1m, AW) last month; however, he's been hard to win with on grass and looks vulnerable back up in grade.
runners.comment: Ran okay when third of six at Southwell last month but looks vulnerable back up in grade

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has returned in top form this term, finishing third at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) then winning the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago; effective over C&D; could be bang there once more up 3lb for last time.
runners.comment: Won the Thirsk Hunt Cup 12 days ago and could be bang there once more up 3lb

runners.name: Duke's Command
runners.spotlight: Two 1m wins on turf last season and added another on AW at Lingfield (1m2f) in December; however, he needs to produce a bigger performance than he's managed across his four outings this year.
runners.comment: Three wins in 2025 but has to produce a bigger run than he's managed so far this year

runners.name: Point Of Contact
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts last July-September after moving into handicaps, the best run coming over C&D (good to firm) in August when close fifth of 17; gelded since last seen and it's possible this lightly raced 4yo will have more to offer for his good yard this term.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who has been gelded and may have more to offer for his good yard

runners.name: Diego Ventura
runners.spotlight: 7f Listed winner at Longchamp last May and he ran some good races subsequently in Group/Listed contests; below par on last month's reappearance at Longchamp but may have needed the run; could go well in first handicap.
runners.comment: Solid performer in Group/Listed races last season; could go well in his first handicap

runners.name: Maybe Not
runners.spotlight: Three wins last June-September (7f-1m) along with 1l second of 18 in a fillies' handicap here (7f, good to firm), and she's better than she showed at Newmarket (1m, good) on her final outing last season; this lightly raced 4yo is in excellent hands to continue to progress and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Three wins last June-September along with a big run here; she's one to consider

runners.name: Galeron
runners.spotlight: On a competitive mark on last year's best efforts but he's without a win since September 2022 and finished merely in mid-division on reappearance in the Lincoln at Doncaster in March (unseated rider on the way to post).
runners.comment: On a competitive mark on last year's best efforts but merely mid-division in the Lincoln

runners.name: Thunder Roar
runners.spotlight: 7yo who was better than ever last season, a campaign that concluded with a fine second of 17 over C&D (good); races off the same mark today but he hasn't shone on his two outings this year.
runners.comment: Fine second off this mark over C&D last October but hasn't shone on either run this year

runners.name: Theoryofeverything
runners.spotlight: Second of 14 off 1lb higher behind Thunder Roar at Ayr (1m) last September on his favoured soft ground, and didn't run badly when 4l 11th of 21 in the Lincoln at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) on his reappearance; however, others have more compelling credentials.
runners.comment: Best form has come on soft ground; midfield in Lincoln on reappearance; others appeal more

runners.name: Croupier
runners.spotlight: Won this for the Crisfords in 2023 when 1lb higher; missed all of 2024 and 2025 and down the field at 40-1 on last month's stable debut at Haydock; he may have needed that run but has to prove how much ability he retains.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023; down the field at Haydock last month when back from very long absence

runners.name: Godwinson
runners.spotlight: Won the Lincoln at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last March and followed up at Newcastle (1m, AW); of interest on those efforts but he went off the boil on his final three starts for William Haggas; needs to get back on track on stable debut (remains in same ownership); slow ground would aid cause.
runners.comment: Last year's Lincoln winner but went off the boil later in the season; has changed yards

runners.name: Point Lynas
runners.spotlight: Below best on final two starts last season and same story on sole run this term, in Qatar in February; also has a hefty mark to defy but this front-runner was a fine second under an enterprising ride in a Group 2 at Ascot last July and has a strong record here, finishing second in this race in 2023 and winning it in 2024; capable of very bold bid from the inside stall.
runners.comment: Strong record at York, including in this race; capable of very bold bid from inside stall
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Vafortino
runners.spotlight: 8yo who is without a win since December 2023 but he showed spark in Dubai this winter and ran big races on his two previous visits to York; returns to a handy on a fair mark and could have an each-way shout.
runners.comment: 8yo who is without a win since December but is worth a second look in view of York form
runners.owner: D MacAuliffe, Anoj Don & Dahab Racing

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: See The Fire
runners.spotlight: Pulverised her seven rivals in this on her second outing of last season, scoring by a whopping 12l on good to firm; couldn't quite reproduce that high-class RPR at Group 1 level in five subsequent outings in 2025 but mostly ran well, including a close second in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, soft); didn't have much to spare at the line when 1-3 for a 1m2f Nottingham Listed race on reappearance but cruised through most of that contest and will likely be a lot closer to her best this time; again the one to beat in this.
runners.comment: Romped home in this last year and the one to beat again on the back of reappearance win

runners.name: Diamond Rain
runners.spotlight: An improved performer last year, winning a good-ground Listed race at Haydock (reappearance) and AW Group 3 at Newcastle around 1m2f before progressing again to place in Grade 1s on firm at Woodbine (1m2f) and Del Mar (1m3f; Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Turf); bang there on form but the fact her top stable has gone nearly three weeks without a winner has to be a slight concern.
runners.comment: Good form shout and capable fresh but top yard quiet in recent weeks

runners.name: Red Letter
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Listed race at Killarney (1m, soft) and Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f, good to yielding) last summer; creditable third of 12 in 1m2f Group 3 back at the Curragh (soft) on reappearance 25 days ago; likely to give a good account but has a bit to find on ratings.
runners.comment: Listed/Group 3 wins last year; solid reappearance; should run well but bit to find on form

runners.name: Fallen Angel
runners.spotlight: Won three 1m Group 1s against her own sex last year, namely the Prix Rothschild, Matron and Sun Chariot (all good/good to yielding); tackles 1m2f for just the second time on her seasonal return (wasn't strong at the finish when fourth in the 2024 Prix de l'Opera on previous attempt but it was only just short of her best RPR at the time); has to carry a 3lb penalty and wasn't at her best on her reappearance in 2024 or 2025; top mare but there are reasons to oppose her this time.
runners.comment: Top mare but the trip, an absence and 3lb penalty are possible reasons to oppose her

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Action
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Westow Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Aspect Island
runners.spotlight: Won Yarmouth nursery (6f, soft) in September but better form when third in Group 3 Cornwallis at Newmarket (good) and Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint (again 5f, firm) the following month; looked to have trained on when beaten around 2l in all-aged Group 3 at Newmarket four weeks ago; holds Group 1 entries over 5f and 6f at Royal Ascot and now drops back into his own age group; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Third in G3 and Grade 1 (again 5f) last term; solid return last month; sets the standard

runners.name: Simplify
runners.spotlight: Improving filly who made all in Thirsk novice (6f, good) on her final start of last term and carried on the good work when making a winning seasonal/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in March; completed the hat-trick with best effort yet in Thirsk handicap (5f, good) last month and she's not discounted in her bid for the four-timer representing last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Improving filly who's not discounted in bid for four-timer from last year's winning yard

runners.name: Revival Power
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last year, making all in Listed event over C&D (good to firm) at the Ebor meeting in August and Doncaster's Group 2 Flying Childers (5f, good) three weeks later; holds a Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot and her sister Winter Power, also a Group-race winning juvenile, won this race in 2021 before landing the Nunthorpe later that year; carries a penalty on this return but a bold bid looks likely.
runners.comment: Won C&D Listed and G2 (5f, good) last term; holds G1 entry; penalised but a big player

runners.name: Wor Faayth
runners.spotlight: Made all in Hamilton maiden (5f, good) in June and won French Listed race (6f, good) on her next start in July; little went right in the Group 2 Lowther here (good to firm) the following month and wasn't disgraced in Chantilly Group 3 (6f, good to soft) when last seen in September; unexposed at this trip and she's not without interest ahead of her comeback.
runners.comment: 5f winner who won French Listed race (6f, good) in July; not without interest on return

runners.name: Dickensian
runners.spotlight: All starts at 5f on good or good to firm; no wins since his debut success at Musselburgh 13 months ago but he ran a belter when second of 20 in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot; again very useful form when beaten around 2l by Revival Power in Listed C&D event in August and Doncaster Group 2 three weeks later; solid each-way claims on this reappearance.
runners.comment: No win since debut but very useful form when 3rd to Revival Power in Listed/G2; e-w claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Boston Dan
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in four-runner Hamilton maiden (5f, good to soft) but it was a fine effort when second at 66-1 in very valuable sales race at the Ebor meeting (6f, good to firm) and bettered that form when runner-up in Listed event back here (good) in October; floundered on heavy ground next time, however, and down the field in Newmarket handicap on return; work to do even if this track brings out the best in him again.
runners.comment: Has gone well here but work to do even if this track brings out the best in him again

runners.name: Manatee Mehmas
runners.spotlight: 105,000gns yearling whose dam is closely related to 6f Group 2 winner Tasleet; sent off odds-on for his debut in Doncaster novice (5f, good to firm) where he made stylish headway before dispatching his main market rival with ease 19 days ago; goes straight into Listed company and he's a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Sparkling debut in 5f novice (odds-on) 19 days ago; fascinating prospect up in grade

runners.name: Black Star Boy
runners.spotlight: Newbury maiden (good to soft) winner who was gelded after finishing down the field in Listed event here (also 6f) in October; coped well with the drop to 5f when taking good step forward to land Sandown handicap (good) on his reappearance three weeks ago; unexposed and likely capable of better again.
runners.comment: Good step forward on first go at 5f with h'cap win on return; unexposed and can do better

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: Won Beverley maiden (soft; debut) and Chester nursery (good to soft) last term; again scored on first start of the year when taking AW handicap (also 5f) in March; possibly found 6f a stretch again when disappointing at Chester last Wednesday but this looks too warm even if she does get back to her best.
runners.comment: Found 6f a stretch when disappointing at Chester last Wednesday but this looks too warm

runners.name: Palmeira
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but she went close when beaten a neck in Newbury Listed race in August; similar form when seventh to Revival Power in Doncaster Group 2 in September and when sixth of 14 in Bath Listed event (also 5f, good) on last month's return; likely to find a few too good again.
runners.comment: Useful filly but she's 0-11 and likely to find a few too good again

runners.name: Exclamation
runners.spotlight: Raced mainly at 5f; won Doncaster novice (good to soft) last summer and bettered that form when fifth to Revival Power in Doncaster Group 2 (80-1) in September; doubled his tally when producing career best to make all in Chester handicap (good) on Friday; drawn on the wing but could be the sort to run above himself.
runners.comment: Made all in Chester handicap (5f, good) on Friday; could be the sort to run above himself

races.race_name: Frank Whittle Partnership Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Arc Ole Ole
runners.spotlight: Scored comfortably at Bath (1m, soft) last October on second start and narrowly got the better of a serious battle for eight-runner handicap at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) in March on reappearance; raised 4lb but that latest start is solid form and this scopey, long-striding sort, who's well related, looks set for further improvement, including at 1m4f.
runners.comment: Solid form with Doncaster 1m2f win in March; set for further progress, including at 1m4f

runners.name: Heroics
runners.spotlight: Made late headway in two runs at Bath (1m, good to soft/soft) in October and built on that when second of eight in novice at Southwell (1m3f, AW) two weeks ago; this is a warm race for handicap debut but it looks as if he will stay at least 1m4f.
runners.comment: Late headway as 2yo and 1m3f second at Southwell two weeks ago; warm race for h'cap debut

runners.name: Sudbury Hill
runners.spotlight: Best efforts to take minor honours in handicaps for new stable on all three starts this season, over 1m2f on last two occasions; lost out in a sustained duel at Redcar (good to firm) last time but kept on well; not the most stoutly bred and today's extra distance needs to spark some improvement.
runners.comment: Minor honours in handicaps for new stable on all three starts this season, 1m2f last two

runners.name: Hard To Believe
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key debut on heavy ground last September but landed a gamble at Bath (1m, good to soft) the following month and made all when odds-on again in a five-runner novice at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in March; the latter performance was on the workmanlike side but gave plenty of encouragement for this longer trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Novice wins on the last two of three starts; encouragement for this trip on handicap debut
runners.owner: Jayar Investments & Mrs L E Ramsden

runners.name: Tommo's Ginjaninja
runners.spotlight: Second or third in four of his five starts, all this year; latest was his turf and handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago when third of nine to Cotton Bud; not bred for this far.
runners.comment: Third to Cotton Bud on turf/handicap debut at Doncaster last time; not bred for this far

runners.name: Cotton Bud
runners.spotlight: Steady progress in four starts for Kevin Philippart De Foy as 2yo, before sold for £26,000gns; 11-1 for nine-runner handicap at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago and came from last to first to get off the mark at first time of asking for new connections; up 5lb but 1m4f should be within reach and she probably has more to give.
runners.comment: Last to first to get off the mark at Doncaster (1m2f) for new connections; more to give

runners.name: Stoneacre Donny
runners.spotlight: 33-1 winner in a novice at Thirsk (7f, soft) last September, coming from towards rear; improved form stepping up in trip when third in his two handicaps since, latest when 25-1 in seven-runner race over 1m4f at Doncaster (good to firm) on reappearance; may be able to build on that.
runners.comment: Third in both handicaps since winning novice at Thirsk; latest was 1m4f; can build on that

runners.name: Pendella
runners.spotlight: Made her debut in December and got off the mark in a Wolverhampton maiden (8.6f, AW) in February; while third of six on handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) was creditable, she clearly needs a lot better than that; should stay beyond 8.6f and her dam was a Listed-placed 10.6f-1m4f winner.
runners.comment: Creditable 3rd of 6 at Southwell on handicap debut but needs more; should stay beyond 8.6f

runners.name: Dryburgh
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden, with new yard this season; upped to 1m2f, hooded and came from the rear on last two outings when close third to Arc Ole Ole at Doncaster (good to soft) and second of eight at Nottingham (good); shapes as if he'll get 1m4f, despite speedy family.
runners.comment: 0-8 but placed at Doncaster and Nottingham last two outings; shapes as if he'll get 1m4f

runners.name: Wicksey
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in two turf races on good last July; firmly on the up in AW handicaps when last seen, registering a quickfire hat-trick in February and last two starts were at 1m4f (made all last time); absent since and this is a much more competitive contest for his return to turf.
runners.comment: Tailed off in two turf races last July; absent since hat-trick in AW handicaps in February

runners.name: Baltic Fleet
runners.spotlight: Short-head winner of a Yarmouth maiden (1m1f, good to soft) in September on second start; couldn't get near the winner in a novice at Bath (1m2f, good) on reappearance but made late gains, suggesting this trip will help on handicap debut; also gelded the day after; needs a second look.
runners.comment: Late gains for 1m2f novice 2nd, suggesting this trip will help on handicap debut; gelded

runners.name: Parisian Scholar
runners.spotlight: Often front-runner; 3-4 in handicaps last term; back with two creditable supporting roles in five-runner handicaps at Kempton (1m3f, AW) when it looked like he would be suited by 1m4f+, but he failed to prove that when never dangerous and well beaten at Chester (good) last week with cheekpieces first enlisted; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Failed to prove suitability for 1m4f when well beaten at Chester last week in cheekpieces

runners.name: Charles Darnay
runners.spotlight: Made his debut in January and all three starts have been on AW; upped from 1m to 1m4f when 11-10 favourite for nine-runner maiden at Kempton ten weeks ago on latest start, responding well to sustained pressure to get up in the final strides; while this handicap/turf debut demands more, he should retain potential for 1m4f+.
runners.comment: Strong late for 1m4f maiden win at Kempton ten weeks ago; should retain potential at 1m4f+

runners.name: Northern Brave
runners.spotlight: Ran twice over 1m at Ayr last autumn, on second occasion leading over 1f out and stretching 5l clear when 2-7 in a four-runner novice (heavy); this warm handicap is a very different assignment, while some of today's rivals look more stoutly bred, but he's unexposed and promising.
runners.comment: Two 1m races at Ayr last autumn, winning easily on second start; unexposed and promising

runners.name: Knightsail
runners.spotlight: Third of seven at Goodwood (1m1f, good) last October on second start and similar form when seventh of nine in another novice at Nottingham (1m2f, good) on reappearance five weeks ago; the latter display failed to live up to his market billing (9-2) but he's a strong candidate for improvement over this longer trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: A strong candidate for improvement over this longer trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Howsham
runners.spotlight: Had four races as 2yo, showing easily best form when triple-figure odds over 1m at Pontefract (good to soft) and York (good) on last two starts; straight up to 1m4f (dam 1m4f winner) for this handicap/seasonal debut under 7lb claimer and he's one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Best form as 2yo in latest two races; one to keep an eye on for this handicap debut

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence - Whips To Be Carried But Not Used)

runners.name: Optimatum
runners.spotlight: Won at Leicester (1m, good to firm) last June when trained by Charlie Johnston and he went close at Southwell (1m, AW) on his final start for that yard; beat only one rival home on his stable debut 174 days ago and now has his first try over 6f; cheekpieces left off.
runners.comment: 1-12; beat only one rival home on stable debut (1m) and now tries 6f for the first time

runners.name: Forever My Prince
runners.spotlight: Signed off 2025 with a claiming success at Leicester (7f, soft) in October on his final start for Eve Johnson Houghton; two fair efforts over 7f since joining this stable and now drops back in trip on ground that he could probably do with being a bit softer than forecast.
runners.comment: Won a 7f claimer on final start for his previous yard; two fair efforts for this stable

runners.name: Arabian Cobra
runners.spotlight: Won at Thirsk (6f, good) last June before following up at Chester (6f, good) a week later; well beaten in the Ayr Bronze Cup on his final start for Hugo Palmer and he beat only one rival home at Bath on his stable debut 27 days ago; visor back on (replacing cheekpieces worn last time).
runners.comment: Dual winner last June; beat only one home on his stable debut 27 days ago; visor returns

runners.name: Ararat
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won twice over 6f at Southwell last year when trained by Robert Cowell, the most recent coming in October off 2lb higher; visor worn on his final start for that yard is left off for this stable debut (bought for 9,500gns).
runners.comment: Won twice at Southwell (AW) over 6f last year; back on turf for this stable debut

runners.name: Safari Dream
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner last year (including this race), the most recent coming off a 2lb higher mark than today's; fair third at Kempton (7f) on his return and ran a solid race when fourth at Bath 27 days ago; claims back at this track (form figures here read 3114).
runners.comment: Two fair efforts since returning from a break; form figures here read 3114; holds claims

runners.name: Distant Rumble
runners.spotlight: Two wins from 18 starts; dropping to 5f wouldn't have suited at Lingfield in February but he was too free at Kempton (6f) when last seen 78 days ago; switches back to turf with others preferred.
runners.comment: Two wins from 18 starts; too free at Kempton when last seen 78 days ago; others preferred

runners.name: Strike
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner from 40 starts; made an encouraging start to the campaign when second at Bath last month but ran below his best at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 10 days ago; without a win since July 2024 and needs to bounce back from that latest effort.
runners.comment: Ran well at Bath on his return but was below his best at Windsor; needs to bounce back
runners.owner: S Emmet And Miss R Emmet

runners.name: Land Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Her four wins have included one over this C&D (good to soft) and she's now 1lb below the most recent of those successes; left the impression her first start for 192 days was needed when weakening at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 10 days ago and she should appreciate this drop in grade; contender.
runners.comment: C&D winner who left impression her reappearance was needed at Windsor; now drops in grade

runners.name: Mumayaz
runners.spotlight: Had a luckless passage at Bath last month but had a clearer run through when winning at Leicester (6f, good to firm) five days ago, travelling strongly and doing it comfortably; carries a 4lb penalty for that success and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Luckless at Bath but made amends with a cosy win at Leicester; looks the one to beat

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gallivanted
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner for this yard; resumed Flat career with close third at Thirsk (1m4f) on handicap debut in this sphere last month and justified strong support when narrowly beating Cruden over this trip at Goodwood (also good) a fortnight ago; unexposed and the best is probably yet to come.
runners.comment: Justified support when narrowly beating Cruden over 1m6f latest; unexposed; key player

runners.name: Just An Hour
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who last scored over 1m2f at Windsor (good to firm) off just 1lb lower in July; runner-up over C&D in October, however, and confirmed this trip holds no fears when second again at Nottingham (also good) 18 days ago; player.
runners.comment: Last won at 1m2f but runner-up over C&D last term and again over 1m6f 18 days ago; player

runners.name: Personal Best
runners.spotlight: Won sole start at two (heavy) and a series of solid efforts from 1m3f to 1m6f last year included win at Ffos Las (1m6f, soft; acts on good) in June; rain would likely increase her chances but still respected on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Solid efforts last year and won (1m6f, soft; acts on good) in June; respected on return
runners.owner: Hot To Trot 1 & Mrs R Pease

runners.name: Tryfan
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner whose sole success on turf came over C&D (good to firm) just under one year ago; also scored off this mark on AW (1m6f) in September but has been very in and out on last four starts, including when tried in a hood (removed) and beaten 32l in valuable AW event last time; drops back down in grade from lower turf mark and can make presence felt if on a going day.
runners.comment: Sole turf win came over C&D (good to firm); inconsistent of late; threat if on a going day

runners.name: Anniversary
runners.spotlight: Won Newmarket maiden on his debut in 2024 but hasn't kicked on from good juvenile form; fair efforts when fifth in quite valuable AW handicaps in February and March but he proved a disappointing favourite on return to turf when beaten 14l at Beverley (also 2m, good to soft) last month; others hold greater appeal.
runners.comment: Fair efforts in quite valuable AW h'caps in February/March but disappointed on turf latest

runners.name: Cruden
runners.spotlight: Won maiden Flat and hurdle race for Jessie Harrington in 2024; big run for this yard when keeping on well from the rear to give Gallivanted a fright at Goodwood (1m6f, good; 25-1) a fortnight ago; had to come down the outer that day and it's no guarantee his old adversary will confirm those placings.
runners.comment: Big run for yard when giving Gallivanted a fright (1m6f, good; 25-1) latest; contender

runners.name: Mr Freedom
runners.spotlight: Six-time Flat winner who scored a fifth win on turf when taking Sandown handicap (1m6f, good) off 2lb lower in July 2024; also a multiple winner over hurdles who was on the premises when taking heavy last-flight fall at Plumpton just over one year ago; interesting to see how he goes in the market ahead of this comeback.
runners.comment: Won (1m6f) off 2lb lower in July 2024; heavy hurdle fall when last seen; market helpful

runners.name: Individualiste
runners.spotlight: Winner over hurdles for Paul Nicholls and fences for Gavin Cromwell but pulled up at big prices in both hurdle starts for this yard; visor deployed on his belated handicap debut in this sphere.
runners.comment: Pulled up both h'dle starts for this yard; visor on for belated h'cap debut in this sphere

runners.name: Alfred Boucher
runners.spotlight: Won sole visit here (1m handicap in 2019); in the care of Ian Williams when landing 2m York handicap in August 2022 and ran a mighty race when pipped in the Ebor a fortnight later; pulled up at Chester the following month and seen just three times since, including when down the field in two starts for this yard last year; drops in grade from falling mark; watch the market on return.
runners.comment: Lightly raced since 2022 but on a dangerous mark; watch the market on this return

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: On The Queue Tee
runners.spotlight: March foal by Massaat; second foal; sister to 8.6f AW 2yo winner Snaafy (RPR 65); dam US 5.5f turf 2yo winner, half-sister to Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner Frescobaldi, out of maiden half-sister to Group-placed 6f/1m2f winner Silver Grey; stable is 0-7 with its 2yos this year; market may provide some clues ahead of this introduction.
runners.comment: March foal by Massaat; stable is 0-7 with its 2yos this year; heed any market clues

runners.name: American Psycho
runners.spotlight: March foal by Mehmas; £130,000 breeze-up 2yo; eighth foal; half-brother to four winners including Space Cowboy (6f AW 2yo; RPR 100), Ikagai (7f 2yo; 95) and Sayem (7f-1m including 2yo; 86); dam unraced half-sister to Japanese 1m Grade 2 winner Mikki Charm, out of 6f Listed winner; plenty of appeal on paper and yard already 4-9 with its juveniles this year; likely debutant.
runners.comment: March foal by Mehmas; £130,000 breeze-up 2yo; yard already 4-9 with its 2yos this year

runners.name: Mobadir
runners.spotlight: Early February foal by No Nay Never; first foal; dam 7f AW/1m turf winner (RPR 100), sister to 6f/7f winner Foreign Diplomat, half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Be My Gal, is granddaughter of 5f Group 3 winner; the oldest runner in the field and respected yard sent out a winning newcomer at Nottingham on Saturday.
runners.comment: Early February foal by No Nay Never; the oldest runner in the field; respected yard

runners.name: Oakford
runners.spotlight: 78,000euros March foal by Dark Angel; 60,000gns yearling; fourth foal; closely related to 5.7f/6f winner Emporess (including 2yo; RPR 72), half-brother to 5f AW 2yo winner Sound And Vision (75); dam placed 5f/7f 2yo (73), half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Empowering, out of 7f Listed winner; first 2yo runner of the season for this yard; heed any market signals.
runners.comment: 78,000euros March foal by Dark Angel; 60,000gns yearling; watch the betting

runners.name: Orthodox
runners.spotlight: 175,000gns late February foal by Havana Grey; 175,000euros yearling; third foal; half-brother to German 1m winner Hello Sweetheart; dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 92), sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Unbreak My Heart, half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Heartache; the most expensive of this field at auction and yard always to be feared in these events.
runners.comment: 175,000gns late February foal by Havana Grey; the most expensive of this field at auction

runners.name: The Bookkeeper
runners.spotlight: 38,000euros early March foal by Sioux Nation; £75,000 yearling; third foal; dam Listed-placed 7f winner (including 2yo; RPR 97), closely related to 7f-9.4f winner Swift Emperor, half-sister to useful 1m-1m2f winner London Icon, out of unraced half-sister to 5f Listed winner Dairine's Delight; yard among the winners with its juveniles; betting likely to provide a good guide to this newcomer's prospects.
runners.comment: 38,000euros early March foal by Sioux Nation; £75,000 yearling; betting a good guide

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Wareeth
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars colt who cost 510,000gns as a yearling; looked a nice prospect when scoring by 14l on his debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March but found the step up to Listed company too much in the Feilden Stakes 29 days ago, albeit he was keeping on again at the line which bodes well for this slightly longer trip; carries a penalty but sets the standard returned to a novice.
runners.comment: Looked a nice prospect on his debut but found Listed company too much latest; up to 1m2f

runners.name: Ancestor
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Sea The Stars; 200,000gns yearling; third foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Reach For The Moon (7f 2yo Group 3) and Chalk Stream (Australian 1m4f Listed), out of 7f Listed winning sister to Oaks runner-up Flight Of Fancy; stable won this last year with Arabian Force; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 200,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; gelded; stable won this last year with Arabian Force

runners.name: Pacific Storm
runners.spotlight: Zarak colt who cost 100,000euros as a yearling; ninth foal; closely related to French 1m4f winner Parabellum (RPR 108), half-brother to three winners including Paix (French 1m4f-1m7f including Group 3; 104) and Psara (French 1m4f; 96); dam French 1m2f-1m7f winner (including Group 3; 109); yard won this in 2024 and there's lots to like on paper.
runners.comment: 100,000euros yearling; dam French 1m2f-1m7f winner (inc Group 3); stable won this in 2024

runners.name: Brother Jack
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to 11.6f-2m winner High Wells (RPR 87); dam Group-placed 6f/7f winner (101), half-sister to useful 5f/6f winner Sohraab and useful 7f/1m winner Pango; well held in a bumper at Kempton (2m, good) 61 days ago and now switches to the Flat; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well held in a bumper at Kempton in March and now switches to the Flat; others preferred

runners.name: Who's Lope
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Who's Glen (RPR 100) who showed his best form at 2m/2m2f; looked in need of the experience on his debut at Kempton (1m, AW; 22-1) in December and now steps up in trip having since been gelded; market should be informative.
runners.comment: Looked in need of the debut experience at Kempton (1m) in December; since been gelded

runners.name: Ramli
runners.spotlight: Third in a bumper at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month when trained by Michael Keady and he didn't run badly when fifth on his Flat/stable debut at Southwell (1m4f, AW) 19 days ago; drops in trip and may be one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Third in a bumper and didn't run badly on his Flat debut at Southwell; drops in trip

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pierian
runners.spotlight: Dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (including Irish Oaks); showed plenty and was beaten only a neck by her stablemate on her debut at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm; 5-1) 18 days ago; nicely bred and she looks a lively contender.
runners.comment: Nicely bred and was beaten only a neck on her debut at Wetherby; looks a lively contender
runners.owner: Lord N P V J Rothschild

runners.name: Alma Latina
runners.spotlight: Dam 1m2f Listed winner; showed plenty of promise after a slow start when third of 10 behind a nice prospect at Sandown (1m2f, good; 33-1) 20 days ago; open to progress and she's interesting for a stable that won this in 2022 and 2024.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of promise when third at Sandown on debut; stable won this in 2022 and 2024

runners.name: Blue Noon
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth of 12 on her debut at Leicester (7f, good to soft) in September and filled that same position on her reappearance at Newmarket (1m2f, good; raced freely) 13 days ago; may yet do better.
runners.comment: Fourth at Leicester (7f) on her debut and over this trip at Newmarket; may yet do better

runners.name: Take A View
runners.spotlight: Sister to 7f/1m2f 2yo winner One World (RPR 91), half-sister to winners Time Lock (1m4f including Group 3; 113) and Continuite (1m; 93); shaped with plenty of encouragement when third on her debut at Newbury (1m2f, good; 33-1) 27 days ago and looks likely to improve; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Shaped with plenty of encouragement when third at Newbury on debut; open to progress

runners.name: Aukus
runners.spotlight: 115,000gns yearling; sister to 1m 2yo/1m2f winner Herring Island; didn't show much and finished tailed off on her debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW; 28-1) 36 days ago; needs to leave that effort well behind in first-time blinkers; stable also runs Alma Latina.
runners.comment: 115,000gns yearling; tailed off on her debut at Kempton last month; blinkers now applied

runners.name: Persian Flaw
runners.spotlight: 3yo by Al Kazeem; third foal; half-sister to 1m4f AW winner Arazzo (RPR 76); dam 1m4f/1m6f winner (84), closely related to 10.5f Listed winner Alkandora, half-sister to useful 1m winner Persian Royal, out of Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner; interesting on her debut for a trainer who runs three; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 1m4f AW winner Arazzo; dam 1m4f/1m6f winner; watch the betting on debut

runners.name: Krystyna
runners.spotlight: 6yo by The Gurkha; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Julie's Love (US 1m/1m2f stakes) and Julissima (German 1m), out of unraced half-sister to Melbourne Cup winner Rekindling and Irish Derby runner-up Golden Sword; best watched on her belated debut.
runners.comment: 6yo by The Gurkha out of an unraced half-sister to Julie's Love and Julissima

runners.name: Sullom Voe
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners including Patrick Sarsfield (1m-1m2f including Group 3; RPR 114), Oz Legend (1m-1m4f; 89) and Tooreen Katie (1m4f; 82); never dangerous when eighth on her debut at Sandown (1m2f, good; 80-1) 20 days ago; open to improvement; stable runs three.
runners.comment: Never dangerous when eighth on her debut at Sandown 20 days ago; open to improvement

runners.name: Liveinthelight
runners.spotlight: Always behind on her debut at Ascot (1m, soft) in September but showed more when fifth of eight at Kempton (1m3f, AW) 45 days ago; that form hasn't worked out and she may be one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Showed more than she did on her debut when fifth at Kempton; looks one for handicaps
runners.owner: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster

runners.name: Palomezcal
runners.spotlight: Dam 6f 2yo and 1m3f (German Oaks) winner (RPR 102); sent off 150-1 when beating only one rival home on her debut at Sandown (1m2f, good; behind Alma Latina) 20 days ago and needs to have taken a big step forward; one of three for her stable.
runners.comment: 150-1 when beating only one rival home at Sandown on her debut; one of three for her yard

runners.name: Counter Intuitive
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns yearling; never featured after a tardy start on her debut at Kempton (1m, AW; 17-2) 190 days ago and has been off since; bred to be suited by this longer trip and she may do better.
runners.comment: 300,000gns; never featured on her debut at Kempton in November; off since and up in trip

races.race_name: Salisbury Annual Badgeholders Handicap

runners.name: Market House
runners.spotlight: Doubled his tally over hurdles when winning in a first-time visor at Plumpton last month but he was below his best back on the Flat at Goodwood (1m6f, good) 13 days ago; drops back down in grade with a place chance.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles last month but below his best on the Flat latest; back down in grade

runners.name: Fair Dinkum
runners.spotlight: His two wins last year include one here over 1m6f in October and he was runner-up off 2lb higher than today's mark at Kempton (2m, AW) next time; respectable fifth at Goodwood 13 days ago but he isn't certain to be suited by this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Won here over 1m6f last year; respectable effort latest but not sure to be suited by 1m4f

runners.name: Kotari
runners.spotlight: His three wins were all on the Flat in 2024, including here on heavy ground over 1m2f; failed to build on his comeback run when well beaten over hurdles at Kempton 52 days ago and the hood returns on this switch back to the Flat; makes some appeal from a good mark.
runners.comment: Well beaten over hurdles last time but he's on a good mark returned to the Flat

runners.name: Shaffron
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability in five starts last year but she didn't help her chance by hanging right at Southwell (1m4f, AW) on her reappearance 16 days ago; up in grade.
runners.comment: Didn't help her chance by hanging right on her return at Southwell; steps up in grade

runners.name: Stage Show
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 32 starts came over hurdles at Exeter (2m7f, good to firm) in October but he's been held in three starts since and now returns to the Flat for the first time since June 2024.
runners.comment: Sole win came over hurdles in October; back on the Flat for the first since since 2024

runners.name: Zhang Fei
runners.spotlight: He's struggled over hurdles since rejoining this stable but he ran a respectable race at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) 10 days ago; needs to build on that effort now stepping back up in trip and he's without a win on the Flat since 2022.
runners.comment: Respectable run at Windsor last time but he's without a win on the Flat since 2022

races.race_name: Molson Coors Handicap

runners.name: Rogue Rebellion
runners.spotlight: Sole win from eight starts came at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January on his second start for James Owen; stepped forward from his debut for this yard when beaten a neck at Bath (5.5f, good) ten days ago and should have no issue with the return to 6f.
runners.comment: Stepped forward from his yard debut when beaten a neck over 5.5f at Bath; back up to 6f

runners.name: Frankali
runners.spotlight: Beaten 3l when third at Kempton (7f, AW) in February but blew her chance at the start next time; didn't get home at Wolverhampton (7f) 17 days ago, having duelled with the leader, and now drops back to 6f for her turf debut, which could suit.
runners.comment: Didn't get home at Wolverhampton last time having duelled with the leader; back to 6f

runners.name: Apex Star
runners.spotlight: Improved form in handicaps since being gelded, finishing third in two outings at Wolverhampton over 7f, most recently nine days ago; makes turf debut but doesn't look certain to benefit from this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Finished third in two starts at Wolverhampton (7f) since being gelded; drops back in trip

runners.name: Prancy Peer
runners.spotlight: 0-7; promise in handicaps on the AW but below her best at Kempton (7f, AW) 45 days ago; now 2lb lower and drops back to 6f; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has shown promise in AW handicaps but below her best last time; drops back in trip

runners.name: Stock Market
runners.spotlight: Ex-Aidan O'Brien; struggled on his first four starts for this stable but ran better when third in the returning blinkers at Windsor (5f, good to firm) ten days ago; steps back up to 6f and looks a contender.
runners.comment: Struggled on his first four starts for this yard but ran better at Windsor last time

runners.name: Dusk Damsel
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability in three maiden runs over further in March and she ran well when runner-up behind a progressive rival at Lingfield (6f, AW) on handicap debut 16 days ago; claims if backing that up on her first start on turf.
runners.comment: Ran well on her handicap debut when runner-up to a progressive rival; now switches to turf
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Sun Path
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three novice runs at Kempton and it was the same story on his handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) 16 days ago; makes his turf debut with better needed.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three novice runs at Kempton and on his handicap debut; makes turf debut

runners.name: Fullstop
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward under more positive tactics when third of 17 at Leicester (7f, good; first-time hood) last month and ran another respectable race at Kempton (7f) two weeks ago; may appreciate this drop back to 6f.
runners.comment: Has done better with recent more positive tactics over 7f; may appreciate the drop to 6f

runners.name: Wadirumm
runners.spotlight: Faded into third at Southwell (7f, AW) on her second start and ran to a similar level when fifth of nine at Lingfield (7f, AW) 24 days ago; now makes her handicap/turf debut and could be suited by the drop in trip.
runners.comment: Ran to a similar level over 7f at Southwell and Lingfield; could be suited by this trip

runners.name: Maid In Devon
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise when fourth of eight on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in March and ran respectably when fifth of 17 at Nottingham (5f, good) 26 days ago; steps back up in trip with place claims.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise on her handicap debut and ran respectably over 5f at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Runamara
runners.spotlight: Sole win from ten starts came in a maiden at Bath (5.5f, soft) in September when trained by Kevin Philippart de Foy; solid effort when runner-up at Kempton last month but he was reported to have run flat at Bath four days later; could bounce back.
runners.comment: 1-10; ran a solid race at Kempton last month but reported to have run flat four days later

runners.name: Creciente
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who pulled too hard when midfield on her reappearance at Leicester (7f, good) 31 days ago; she may come on for that outing and this drop back to 6f may help.
runners.comment: 0-6; pulled too hard at Leicester (7f) on last month's return but this drop to 6f may help

runners.name: Pink Diamond Girl
runners.spotlight: Raced freely before weakening into seventh on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f) in March, and she again failed to settle when beating only one rival home there nine days ago; drops in trip for turf debut.
runners.comment: Has been too free in two 7f handicaps on the AW and now drops in trip for her turf debut

runners.name: Follow My Heart
runners.spotlight: Big prices and beaten about 12l in each of her three runs earlier in the year (7f/6f); makes her turf/handicap debut having been off for 80 days and she's best watched unless the market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: Beaten about 12l in each of her three runs on the AW earlier in the year; handicap debut

runners.name: Stole My Heart
runners.spotlight: Best effort in three starts for Clive Cox came when a modest sixth at Kempton (7f, AW) in December but she was well beaten there in January; has since switched yards and drops back to 6f for her handicap/turf debut; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Modest sixth at Kempton in December; has left Clive Cox and makes her handicap/turf debut

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Classic Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Scriabin
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; 12-race maiden over hurdles but placed in three of his four for current yard, last time finishing third of 12 in C&D handicap (good) 21 days ago; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden over hurdles but placed on three of four runs for this yard, including C&D

runners.name: Lexington Wood
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three Irish bumpers last year, after which he was sold for £105,000; novicey jumping, in at the deep end, on his hurdles debut in a Grade 2 at Kempton (2m, good to soft) when eighth of ten finishers in February; sure to have been well schooled since and could be a different proposition here.
runners.comment: Promise in bumpers; made hurdles/yard debut in Grade 2; could fare lot better now

runners.name: Kibris
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat winner at the start of his career but failed to progress in juvenile hurdles last season after a promising start; also well held back on the Flat at the Curragh six weeks ago (final start for Joseph O'Brien); more than capable of making his presence felt in a race like this if putting his best foot forward but starts out for a new yard (same owners) with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Big shout at best but ended time with Joseph O'Brien on a low note

runners.name: Struth
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in his prime but has yet to better fair form over hurdles, weakening into a well-held fifth on 2m2f Kelso handicap debut in this sphere 24 days ago; might have needed that after a break but he's still hard to make a good case for.
runners.comment: Useful on Flat in prime but yet to better fair form over hurdles

runners.name: Way Maker
runners.spotlight: Won bumpers at Bangor (good) and Stratford (good to firm) last summer; found life tough under a double penalty in a valuable bumper at Newbury back from a break in March but remains a good hurdles prospect, particularly for this time of year.
runners.comment: Two bumper wins last summer; not disgraced after break latest; respected hurdles newcomer

runners.name: Sonyourastar
runners.spotlight: Reached the frame in three 3m Irish points; well-held fourth on 2m4f Market Rasen hurdles debut (good to soft) in March but it was an okay race, with the three ahead of him all successful since; down in trip now; Sean Bowen on the yard's other runner.
runners.comment: In frame in points; minor promise on hurdles debut but Sean Bowen on yard's other runner

runners.name: Etretat
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful four-time AW Flat winner for Charlie Johnston but 0-17 on turf for that yard; ran poorly at Lingfield (AW) 16 days ago and changed hands for 1,000gns since; best watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Four AW Flat wins but 0-17 on turf; sold for 1,000gns ahead of hurdles debut

runners.name: Hackley Bay
runners.spotlight: 125-1, well held on course bumper debut (good) 20 days ago; can only watch now hurdling.
runners.comment: 25-1, well held on course bumper debut (good) 20 days ago; can only watch now hurdling

runners.name: Brewdog
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two bumpers last season; big outsider now hurdling.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in two bumpers last season; big outsider now hurdling

runners.name: Breaking Ground
runners.spotlight: Signs of only a little ability so far and will need to find significant improvement for the addition of a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Signs of only a little ability and needs to find significant improvement in tongue-tie

runners.name: Hawthorn Road
runners.spotlight: Pulled up having lost ground quickly when 5-2 joint favourite for a Musselburgh bumper in December; hooded when modest seventh of 13 in another bumper at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) 12 weeks later; not an obvious one to strike on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Modest seventh on complete start in bumpers ahead of this switch to hurdles

runners.name: L'Appel Du Large
runners.spotlight: Well held in both bumpers last season, although the fact he was sent off at 5-2 for the first one suggests he must have been showing some promise at home; has had wind surgery ahead of this hurdles debut after two months off.
runners.comment: Well held both bumpers, although he was only 5-2 for debut; had wind op; hurdles debut

runners.name: Nae Bother At A'
runners.spotlight: Poor form in seven outings over hurdles and needs to show significant improvement in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Poor form and needs to show significant improvement in a first-time hood

races.race_name: DM Hall Surveyors Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Largy Force
runners.spotlight: Progressive mare who has form figures of 21212 since joining Olly Murphy; edged out by a next-time-out winner (pair 19l clear) at Uttoxeter (3m, good to soft) last time and still looks well treated after a 3lb nudge.
runners.comment: Record of 21212 since joining Olly Murphy; form of latest second boosted; leading claims

runners.name: Busty Boy
runners.spotlight: Only 1-22 under rules and clearly hard to win with but he arrives in form having placed in a second successive Highland National here (3m6f, good) last month; just as effective over this shorter distance.
runners.comment: Hard to win with (1-22) but regularly placed, including in Highland National here latest

runners.name: Inis Oirr
runners.spotlight: Back to form a little out of the blue to take advantage of a reduced mark over C&D (good) under Gregor Walkinshaw three weeks ago; remains well treated on old form (won 2024 Edinburgh National off 11lb higher) but not sure to be in the same mood again based on his recent record.
runners.comment: Back to form with a C&D win last month but not sure to be in the same mood again

runners.name: Red Happy
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at around 3m in February/March 2025 but not seen again until refusing to race when a 20-1 shot at Kelso 24 days ago; can only watch after that.
runners.comment: Landed hat-trick in early 2025 but refused to race on belated return at Kelso 24 days ago

runners.name: Ned Tanner
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 2m4f on soft ground in 2024-25 season but has been rather inconsistent and didn't offer enough when fifth of seven over C&D (good) three weeks ago to suggest he's likely to cash in on his reduced mark now.
runners.comment: Now lower than for both wins in 2024-25 but not obviously in the form to take advantage

runners.name: Cosmic Blizzard
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish pointer; good second in a Kelso novice handicap chase (2m7f, good) last autumn but has pulled up on his two outings since (reportedly bled latest); something to prove.
runners.comment: Good second at Kelso last autumn but pulled up both runs since
runners.owner: County Set Four & L V Russell

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Hugo Spritz Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Sun Art
runners.spotlight: Won in first-time tongue-tie at Ludlow (2m, novice; good) in March 2025; placed in three of his five starts since, runner-up to the 13-8 favourite Wild Goose at Hereford (2m, good) four weeks ago; stamina not yet proven over 2m4f; possible.
runners.comment: Won in first-time tongue-tie at Ludlow (2m) in March 2025; runner-up latest; stamina 2m4f?

runners.name: Moyganny Phil
runners.spotlight: Former pointer; consistent over hurdles, ridden out to see off big-priced rival at Carlisle (2m4f, good to soft; 7-4 joint-favourite) over Easter; fairly treated off this 2lb higher mark and, with the prospect of more to come, rates as the pick of the Olly Murphy trio.
runners.comment: Ridden out to see off big-priced rival at Carlisle (2m4f) over Easter; 2lb higher; player.

runners.name: Bold Light
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 2 at Kelso (2m5f, good to soft; from 7lb higher) in March 2025, but has failed to match that level of form since (0-9); wasn't beaten too far over C&D (Class 3; fourth of seven) three weeks ago, and a similar effort would give him a shout in this added tongue-tie; 2-10 for Alan Doyle.
runners.comment: Fourth of seven over C&D recently; similar effort would give him a shout; tongue-tie on.

runners.name: Carismatic Soldier
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start over hurdles, keeping on well to score at Chepstow (2m novice, heavy; 6-5) in January 2025; failed to progress handicapping (0-6) and continues to ease in the weights, tried in various forms of headgear; soundly beaten here (3m2f) on Wednesday.
runners.comment: Won a Chepstow novice but disappointing in handicaps; soundly beaten here (3m2f) Wednesday

runners.name: Follow Charlie
runners.spotlight: 4-29, including a C&D win (from 2lb lower) at this two-day Perth fixture in 2025; has been competing over 3m recently, failing to get home when fourth to Suspected (beaten over 13l) here three weeks ago; dropping back in trip is in his favour; don't discount hastily.
runners.comment: Failed to get home when fourth (btn 13l) over 3m here recently; drop back to 2m4f a plus.

runners.name: Dubhain
runners.spotlight: Point winner, who was also twice placed in bumpers; first two attempts over hurdles (maidens) were satisfactory but he was well beaten at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yielding to soft) six weeks ago; hood added to tongue-tie for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Fairyhouse (2m4f) six weeks ago; hood added to tongue-tie for h'cap debut.

runners.name: Summergrounds
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over C&D in 2025 and filled that same position behind Kingston Narcissus at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft) on Boxing Day; pulled up on return from break at Ayr (2m4f, soft) latest; may strip fitter, and it's a positive that Danny McMenamin is back aboard.
runners.comment: Pulled up on return from break at Ayr (2m4f) latest; strip fitter?; McMenamin back aboard.

runners.name: Easy To Follow
runners.spotlight: On the rise for previous yard in late 2023, winning back-to-back at Uttoxeter (maiden) and Fontwell (novice); mostly disappointing over fences (0-5), pulling up at Huntingdon in January when last seen; back to hurdles from fair mark on first start for Olly Murphy; consult the market.
runners.comment: 0-5 fences; back to hurdles from a fair mark on first start for new yard; consult market.

runners.name: Dalileo
runners.spotlight: Three-time Perth winner (2m4f-3m) who is back on his last winning mark and should have the ground (good) to suit; late headway when fourth of 14 at Ayr (2m4f, soft) last November but well beaten here on Wednesday on his first run since.
runners.comment: Three-time Perth winner (2m4f-3m); well beaten on Wednesday on first run since November
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Hugo Spritz Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Lets Mingle
runners.spotlight: Short-odds C&D maiden winner (four ran) this time last year; form figures since introduced to handicapping read 243U, still bang there when unseating her rider at the last over C&D recently; good ground plays to strengths; possibilities despite going up 4lb.
runners.comment: Still bang there when unseating her rider at the last over C&D recently; now 4lb higher.

runners.name: Kado Sacree
runners.spotlight: Point winner; left previous rules efforts behind, ridden out to make winning handicap debut at Carlisle (2m4f, good to soft; 11-10 favourite) over Easter; that wasn't a particularly deep race, so a 3lb rise is fair; prospect of more to come in newly applied tongue-tie; stablemate of Atlantic City.
runners.comment: Made winning handicap debut at Carlisle (2m4f; 11-10 favourite) over Easter; 3lb higher.

runners.name: Atlantic City
runners.spotlight: Form figures since leaving Ciaran Murphy read 21; produced late to make winning handicap debut over C&D (good; 4-1, from 5lb lower) three weeks ago; James King retains the mount with Sean Bowen booked to ride stablemate Kado Sacree; contender.
runners.comment: Made recent winning C&D handicap debut (from 5lb lower); stablemate of Kado Sacree.

runners.name: Getaway Master
runners.spotlight: The handicapper has cut him slack, and he sits 1lb higher than when winning readily at Carlisle (2m4f, soft) last November; modest this year, flattering briefly before two out over C&D on Wednesday.
runners.comment: 1lb above last winning mark; modest this year, flattering briefly over C&D on Wednesday

runners.name: Largy Meadow
runners.spotlight: Patiently ridden and bustled up the winner (Camarrate) at Newcastle (2m4f, good; from 2lb lower) on penultimate start; pulled up over C&D subsequently; still low mileage, so isn't a forlorn hope if bouncing back from that blip.
runners.comment: Bustled up winner at Newcastle (2m4f, good; from 2lb lower) penultimate; PU over C&D since

runners.name: Kinbara Firstdraft
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles, including here (2m, good to firm; from 9lb lower) in June 2024; hasn't really taken to fences (0-3), only fourth of six at Ayr (2m, soft) three months ago; this return to hurdles could reignite a spark, but it's likely he's a few pounds too high in the handicap.
runners.comment: Return to hurdles could reignite a spark; possibly a few pounds too high in the handicap.

runners.name: Wasdell Dundalk
runners.spotlight: No win over hurdles since July 2021, and was a remote last of eight in this last year; solid record over fences here - four wins at 3m - which does at least offer some hope, even if he has struggled for form (P84) in his last three starts; might need this off a 232-day absence.
runners.comment: Solid record over fences here - four wins at 3m - which does at least offer some hope.
runners.owner: Abbadis Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Annie Agnew
runners.spotlight: Game Down Royal winner; runner-up (no match for winner) over 2m here in June 2024; blown more cold than hot since, pulling up over 3m at Down Royal last October; easing mark but with a bit to prove at present, especially off a 195-day absence.
runners.comment: Runner-up (no match for winner) over 2m here in June 2024; blown more cold than hot since.

runners.name: Sauce Of Life
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in bumpers (2-2) and very lightly raced for his age; seldom seen in action since winning by a neck at Doncaster (2m4f, good to soft; from 3lb lower) in December 2024; fitness is taken on trust but it's noted that connections persevere for the time being.
runners.comment: Seldom seen since winning by a neck at Doncaster (2m4f; from 3lb lower) in December 2024.
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Seaside Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Just Dottie
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year, from this mark, but with Sean Bowen in the saddle; that was her fourth Perth win and she notched again on the Flat at Ayr (1m5f, Class 5, soft) in September; recent C&D comeback run should bring her on but she has work to do with Magnolia (second) and Pure Gold (fifth).
runners.comment: Won this last year, from this mark, but with Sean Bowen in the saddle; repeat is unlikely.

runners.name: Castle Ivers
