runners.spotlight: Looked useful when winning a Wetherby bumper by 7l in March 2024; hurdles career off to a perfect start at Warwick (2m, good to soft) the following season, but absent for 18-plus months thereafter until reappearing at Ayr (4-1 favourite; tailed off) recently; now sits 2lb lower.
runners.comment: Useful bumper & maiden hurdle winner; tailed off on recent comeback run (h'cap debut).

runners.name: Trust House
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick after justifying favouritism at Market Rasen and Ffos Las (both 2m); a combined rise of 16lb - 8lb for latest win - makes life more challenging as he moves into a Class 3 for the first time; however, he's improving and spearheads his yard's triple representation in this field.
runners.comment: On hat-trick after justifying favouritism at Market Rasen & Ffos Las (combined rise 16lb).

runners.name: Pure Gold
runners.spotlight: Couldn't fault her momentum last year, rising from a mark of 88 at Uttoxeter in July to 102 when scoring readily at Hexham (2m, good to soft) in October; found life tougher in better races (Wetherby and Cheltenham) thereafter; this is equally tough, but she could strip fitter for C&D comeback run.
runners.comment: 6lb higher than when winning readily at Hexham (2m) last October; this rates a tough test.
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Magnolia
runners.spotlight: Both British wins have come at Kelso, comfortably seeing off Joltin And Jiving there (2m, soft; 13-8 favourite) last November; slowly edging back down the weights (2lb above last winning mark); second-best to another Olly Murphy-trained runner over C&D (25-1, beaten 11l) latest.
runners.comment: Second-best to another Olly Murphy-trained runner over C&D (25-1, beaten 11l) latest.

runners.name: Oh Janey
runners.spotlight: Always doing enough when making winning handicap debut at Downpatrick (2m1f; 9-4 favourite) last October; well beaten in a Grade 3 mares' novice next time, and absent since; no issue with this track, so mildly interesting to see how she fares off a BHA mark of 109.
runners.comment: Made winning handicap debut at Downpatrick (2m1f; 9-4 favourite) last October; 9lb higher.

runners.name: Double Oban
runners.spotlight: 5-11 over hurdles but the bulk of those wins have come on soft or heavy ground; sits 4lb higher than when winning at Leicester (2m, soft; 9-4, four runners) over Christmas; one of three declared runners for Olly Murphy.
runners.comment: 4lb higher than when winning at Leicester (2m) over Christmas; one of three for O. Murphy.

races.race_name: Edinburgh Cannonball Handicap Chase

runners.name: Traveling Soldier
runners.spotlight: Didn't jump well when pulled up in first-time blinkers at Plumpton last November when last seen; however, he was in good form previously in cheekpieces and they go back on today; could have a big say.
runners.comment: Pulled up in blinkers when last seen but in good form in these cheekpieces previously

runners.name: Sir Carnegie
runners.spotlight: He can make mistakes and may be better over further, but he won two in a row last autumn (2m7f, good/good to soft) and was back on song when runner-up here (3m, good) three weeks ago in reapplied cheekpieces (retained since); good third here (3m, good to soft) yesterday; could be in the mix.
runners.comment: He's run well in defeat over 3m here on his last two starts, most recently yesterday

runners.name: Uncle Al
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) at Sedgefield in December but below par on his next three starts and he unseated early at Hexham 12 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won at Sedgefield in December but hasn't performed to the same level subsequently

runners.name: Carrigeen Castle
runners.spotlight: 10yo who was in and out last season but returned to form when second at Hexham (2m4f, soft) in March last time; could have a part to play if conditions are suitable (he's unraced on good ground under rules).
runners.comment: 10yo who was in and out last season but returned to form when second at Hexham last time

runners.name: Tanking Along
runners.spotlight: Made all at Sedgefield (2m, good) in March and placed on last two starts, most recently runner-up at Kelso (2m1f, good) last week; the step back up in trip poses a question but he's a leading player if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Kelso last week and he's a leading player if seeing out this longer trip

runners.name: Wings On Wheels
runners.spotlight: Point runner-up who improved upon his previous rules form when 5l second of four at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in March when wearing first-time cheekpieces (retained) on his second chase/handicap start; this very lightly raced 7yo may have more to offer and could be involved.
runners.comment: Step in the right direction at Huntingdon on second chase start and he could be involved

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hold The Throw
runners.spotlight: Bought for £72,000 after finishing second in a 3m Irish point last November; has joined a leading stable with a healthy bumper strike-rate, including 2-6 here; obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Second in Irish point and joined leading yard with good bumper record; obvious appeal

runners.name: Killygowan
runners.spotlight: 15,000euros 3yo; half-brother to winners Hurricane Dylan (2m4f-2m6f hurdle/chase) and Mountain Philip (2m7f chase); dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Darceys Dancer; Irish stable has a good 10-54 record in bumpers at Perth; probably the yard's first string here based on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: One of two newcomers from an Irish yard which does well in bumpers here

runners.name: Royalty Row'd
runners.spotlight: Brother to useful 2m3f hurdle/2m4f chase winner Raglan Road; dam 1m5f Flat winner (RPR 78), half-sister to 1m4f Listed Flat and useful 2m hurdle winner Aklan; newcomer from a stable which saddled a 1-2 in a C&D bumper last month; market informative.
runners.comment: Newcomer from a yard which had a 1-2 in C&D bumper last month; market informative

runners.name: Sea Tempest
runners.spotlight: Related to several useful performers and he shaped very nicely when a close third at Southwell (soft; 6-1) 12 weeks ago, doing his best work late on; open to improvement and may be the answer.
runners.comment: Shaped well when third on Southwell debut; open to improvement and may be the answer

runners.name: Saint Cunning
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros half-brother to point winner Brave Starlight; dam unraced half-sister to some useful winner; Killygowan might be the stable first string on jockey bookings but this one still needs checking out in the betting.
runners.comment: Killygowan might be the yard first strong but the betting will provide more clues

runners.name: Ballynulto
runners.spotlight: Remote third on his completed start in Irish points but the fact his trainer has a 5-12 bumper record in British bumpers in the last five seasons, including a win in this in 2024, means he needs monitoring in the betting.
runners.comment: Remote third on completed start in points but his Irish yard does well in British bumpers

runners.name: Busted Springs
runners.spotlight: 80-1 when well beaten on his debut in a bumper at Ayr Scottish Grand National meeting; has a tongue-tie added this time.
runners.comment: Well held on last month's Ayr debut; has a tongue-tie added now

runners.name: Clova Chocolate

runners.name: Another Blunder
runners.spotlight: Beaten 25l in bumpers at Listowel and Cork last summer; can only be watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Beaten 25l in 2 bumpers last summer; can only be watched on hurdles debut

runners.name: Arizona Robbins
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden and well held on both maiden hurdle runs last season.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden and well held on both maiden hurdle runs last season

runners.name: The Fog Horn
runners.spotlight: Two-time Gowran Flat winner (good/yielding); reappeared from winter break at same venue in March when tailed off on hurdles debut on heavy; ground more in her favour here but still best watched.
runners.comment: Heavy ground wouldn't have suited on hurdles bow; better ground but still best watched

races.race_name: Clonmel Racecourse Supporters Club Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Eye Of A Tiger
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner last summer after four consecutive placed efforts; out of her depth subsequently in much stronger company; faller on chase debut at Tramore in January; again set an ambitious task as outsider of three in a conditions hurdle at Limerick in March, this is more realistic.
runners.comment: Has been aimed high since Kilbeggan maiden hurdle win last summer, drops back in class

runners.name: Sense Of Occasion
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point-to-point on good-to-yielding; showed a fair standard of form in maiden hurdles on winter ground; clear of the remainder when second in a six-runner contest at Limerick last month; different ground now, good chance if he handles it well.
runners.comment: Second at Limerick last month but different ground now, good chance if he handles it well

runners.name: Lady Doyanne
runners.spotlight: Point winner; won a 2m1f Wexford maiden hurdle last August; was then poor at Navan on handicap debut; much better but still well beaten at Punchestown on second attempt; finished sixth at Kilbeggan last month, rider weighed in light, disqualified.
runners.comment: Won a 2m1f Wexford maiden last August, stablemate Sense Of Occasion appeals more

runners.name: Chanceawetmorning
runners.spotlight: Best effort for Noel Kelly when second of 12 finishers (beaten favourite) over 2m4f at Fairyhouse in January; flopped when favourite at Ayr in March; justified good support when winning easily at Kilbeggan on first appearance for Gordon Elliott; raised by 9lb, but Josh Williamson's 5lb claim could help him to follow up.
runners.comment: Won easily at Kilbeggan on stable debut, raised by 9lb, rider's 5lb claim is an asset

runners.name: Takarengo
runners.spotlight: No win since 2021; has occasionally run well in defeat; improved from a run on the Flat at Navan in March when going well for this jockey (on her first ride over jumps) at Wexford last month; beaten 8l in fourth, so needs to raise his game another notch.
runners.comment: A long time since his last win, went well for this rider when fourth at Wexford

runners.name: Tingarran Express
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner, also placed here four times; battled well from the front to land a mares' handicap at Tramore last August (good); probably in need of the run on course return in February; likely to strip fitter now; won here on her second start after a break last year; strong contender.
runners.comment: Dual course winner, also placed here four times, probably needed the run in February

runners.name: Theflyingbee
runners.spotlight: Useful mare; went a bit high in the ratings after winning at Downpatrick last June; now back on a mark only one pound higher and holds a big chance in this company on the strength of her third placing in a 24-runner contest at the Punchestown festival.
runners.comment: Holds a big chance on her third placing in a 24-runner contest at the Punchestown festival

runners.name: Chosen Shant
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner of modest handicap hurdles at Wexford last summer; lost form in the autumn and ran poorly at Limerick four weeks ago on his first run after a winter break; better ground could make a big difference now; only 2lb above his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Back-to-back winner of modest handicap hurdles at Wexford last summer, ground should suit

runners.name: Expound
runners.spotlight: Made almost all to win this race last year (Ballycashin third); ran well off 10lb higher mark at Tramore in August; otherwise has done all his racing on the Flat over the past year; placed on multiple occasions, fair form late; fourth of nine at Ballinrobe last Friday.
runners.comment: Made almost all to win this race last year, has been running on the Flat, could go well

runners.name: Tir Og
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well in the early part of last season, rising 30lb in ratings, stemming from three wins and two seconds; probably too high in the ratings now; was unsuited by the race conditions at Killarney last Friday, so should not be judged harshly on that account.
runners.comment: Highly progressive in the early part of last season, probably too high in the ratings now

runners.name: Ballycashin
runners.spotlight: Has recorded her only two wins over hurdles at this venue, both over longer distances than this; stuck to her task over 2m4f here last October; failed to exploit a much lower chase mark here the following month and absent since.
runners.comment: Has recorded her only two wins over hurdles at this venue, this trip is short of her best

runners.name: Sonny May
runners.spotlight: Career highlights when winning back-to-back handicap hurdles at Gowran and Punchestown in November 2024; a long way below his best in a light 2025 campaign that included two poor runs over fences; well held by Theflyingbee on Punchestown running.
runners.comment: A long way below his best in a light 2025 campaign, held by Theflyingbee on recent form

races.race_name: John Walsh (Mainstown) Memorial INH Flat Race

runners.name: It's All Clear
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on the form of soft-ground events at Naas and this venue when fourth at Kilbeggan; 50-1 then, likely to be one of the leading fancies now in what looks a weaker contest; better ground was probably the key to his improvement, and the form could be solid with Gavin Cromwell and Willie Mullins responsible for the placed horses
runners.comment: 50-1 when fourth at Kilbeggan, could be a leading fancy in what looks a weaker contest
runners.owner: Paul M O'Loughlin & W D Gannon

runners.name: Mistakenforanother
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; Free Eagle mare is a sister to a Slovakian winner, half-sister to Mywayistheonlyway, a four-time winner at up 7f; dam US 6f-1m winner.
runners.comment: Flat pedigree, half-sister to Mywayistheonlyway a four-time winner at up 7f

runners.name: Lirakali
runners.spotlight: E40,000 3yo; eighth foal; brother to a Polish jumps winner, half-brother to a French chase winner Miralka; dam French hurdle winner; top stable had three winners over jumps at Killarney on Saturday/Monday; likely a prime contender in an ordinary bumper.
runners.comment: French pedigree, from a leading stable, likely a prime contender in an ordinary bumper

runners.name: Martymill
runners.spotlight: Surpassed market expectations when fourth of 14 at 150-1 on debut at Fairyhouse last November; failed to match that effort at the same venue in January.
runners.comment: Fourth of 14 at 150-1 on debut at Fairyhouse last November, lesser effort on second start

runners.name: Away To Paris
runners.spotlight: An E11,000 3yo; second foal; dam, by Robin Des Champs, unraced half-sister to useful winners Not Many Left, Coverholder and Angus Milan;
runners.comment: Dam by Robin Des Champs an unraced half-sister to three useful winners

runners.name: Black Bog Lad
runners.spotlight: First foal; Mahler gelding; dam was of little account on the track, decent pedigree, out of a winning half-sister to leading French jumper Cyrlight.
runners.comment: Mahler gelding; dam was of little account on the track, decent French pedigree

runners.name: Bobs House
runners.spotlight: Three-figure odds at Thurles and Cork and has run accordingly, tailed off both times; tongue-tie fitted now.
runners.comment: Three-figure odds at Thurles and Cork, tailed off both times, tongue-tie fitted now

runners.name: Mindyourheadmike
runners.spotlight: Made mistakes when pulled up on sole points start; never showed with a chance at Bellewstown last month; in good hands but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Never showed with a chance at Bellewstown last month; in good hands but hard to fancy

runners.name: Hoof And Hustle
runners.spotlight: Finished with only one behind him in a 12-runner Leopardstown bumper won by the subsequent Cheltenham winner The Mourne Rambler; backed at high prices in the morning, sent off 12-1; had obviously shown something and may do much better on good ground.
runners.comment: In rear in a strong bumper at Leopardstown, 12-1 then, may do better on good ground

runners.name: I Give Theorders
runners.spotlight: Placed on point debut for respected producer Ellen Doyle in February 2024; pulled up at Portrush later that year; reported to have burst blood vessels when 14-1 for a bumper at Wexford last August; his new trainer fits him with a hood
runners.comment: Burst blood vessels when 14-1 for a bumper at Wexford last August, new trainer

races.race_name: Mercedes-Benz Of Chichester Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Shutfrontdoor
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; well held when falling heavily on hurdle debut in December but returned from break with two pretty good seconds towards end of last season (both 2m, good); probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Ended last season with two pretty good seconds; probably won't be far away
runners.owner: J Hinds, A Head & G L Moore

runners.name: Hidden Fortune
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who came second in two five-runner bumpers last season (good to soft/good); switches to hurdling with potential but may not be seen to best effect until tackling longer trips.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who came second in two five-runner bumpers last season; hurdle debut

runners.name: Flash Bardot
runners.spotlight: Useful over middle distances on the Flat (five wins) and made promising stable/hurdle debut when third at Haydock (1m7f, good) last month; that run came after a layoff and she'll be dangerous if it brought her on.
runners.comment: Useful Flat racer who made quite promising stable/hurdle debut last month; good chance

runners.name: Diamond White
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on last year's Irish point debut and made the frame in all three subsequent bumpers; no match for clear winner when second at Kelso (good to soft) in March but still enters calculations on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Placed on Irish point debut and made the frame in all three bumpers; considered

runners.name: Secret Des Lune
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when battling well to win five-runner Plumpton bumper (good to soft) in March; likely to encounter faster ground on this hurdling debut but ought to play a significant role.
runners.comment: Battled well to win Plumpton bumper in March; has big role to play on hurdling debut

runners.name: Sorgean
runners.spotlight: Joined Harry Fry after going close in an Irish point in November and made winning rules debut in five-runner Wincanton bumper (good) last month; that form isn't anything to get too excited about but she should have more to offer over hurdles.
runners.comment: Won five-runner Wincanton bumper on rules debut; should have more to offer over hurdles

runners.name: Whodunit
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when eighth of 12 in good-ground Newton Abbot bumper last month, her second start, but still makes only limited appeal on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when eighth in a bumper last month but best watched on hurdle debut

runners.name: Rock Sensation
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground Southwell bumper for Ben Pauling in spring 2025 and made the frame in soft-ground Listed event at Cheltenham in November but didn't get far on hurdling debut in December and was remote when third at Lingfield (2m3f, good to soft) later that month; makes stable debut after a break and with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Looked promising in bumpers but made disappointing start to hurdling career; new stable

runners.name: Saffron Belle
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in both bumpers and unseated her rider after trying to refuse at second flight on hurdle debut; cannot be recommended.
runners.comment: Unseated her rider after trying to refuse at second flight on hurdle debut

runners.name: Queens Up
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when midfield at Chepstow (2m, good) last month but the value of that form is open to question and others in this line-up have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when midfield at Chepstow last month but others look much stronger

races.race_name: KSR Lighting Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Oakley
runners.spotlight: 2-7 for current stable since the autumn, the latter win gained easily by over 8l at Hereford (2m3f, good) nine days ago; not always one to rely upon heavily but has obvious claims under 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Not always reliable but won easily by over 8l at Hereford last week; carries 7lb penalty

runners.name: Sun Joy
runners.spotlight: Back in prime form when last seen in the autumn, winning at Ludlow before running well in defeat at Kempton (also 2m5f, good); contender if ready to roll after six-month break.
runners.comment: Back in prime form when last seen in the autumn; contender if ready to roll after break

runners.name: Motazzen
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who rewarded good market support and showed career-best form when drawing clear to score by 10l here (2m5f, good to soft) in March; 8lb rise asks a fair bit of him but the forecast faster ground should not be a problem.
runners.comment: Career-best form when winning by 10l here in March; 8lb rise asks a fair bit of him

runners.name: Taritino
runners.spotlight: Looked as good as ever when winning readily by 7l at favourite track Taunton (2m3f, good) three weeks ago and is now 3-19 over hurdles; 7lb higher here but still respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Looked as good as ever when winning readily at Taunton in April; respected in current mood

runners.name: Bel Ombre
runners.spotlight: No match for winner when second at Perth (2m4f, good; raced too freely) last month, after a break, but appeared to have quite a bit in hand when winning on handicap debut at Taunton (2m3f, good to soft) in January and can be dangerous if new hood helps him to relax.
runners.comment: No match for winner when second last time but remains very much of interest

runners.name: Belgarum
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner who was out of sorts when last seen in the autumn, most recently when pulled up on return to hurdling; had another wind op (his fourth) during 168-day break; needs to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Out of sorts when last seen in the autumn; needs to get career back on track

runners.name: Khalk'eau Spigao
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise in his three qualifying races last season (2m-2m1f) and is 2lb out of the weights for this handicap debut but improvement is possible at some stage.
runners.comment: Made low-key start to hurdling career but improvement is possible in handicaps
runners.owner: D S Dennis & John Hw Finch

runners.name: Hiconic
runners.spotlight: Came second off basement Flat mark on latest outing in January and is well handicapped for this return to hurdling but she's not as good as she was and has become inconsistent; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Reverts to hurdling on a good mark but has regressed and become inconsistent

races.race_name: Fontwell Park Half Year Membership Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Lunar Ocean
runners.spotlight: Can make mistakes but mainly consistent over fences for this yard; pulled up at Warwick after an error last time but 2m and ground that was likely faster than good were probably not ideal; that brought his record to 0-18 but he's not ruled out in first-time blinkers back up in trip.
runners.comment: Mainly consistent over fences; excuses last time and not ruled out in first-time blinkers

runners.name: Ballyfinn
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough on his fifth start over fences when scoring here (3m2f, good) last Wednesday; back down in trip under 7lb penalty but not fully exposed and looks a key player.
runners.comment: Breakthrough win here (3m2f) last Wednesday; down in trip under 7lb penalty; key candidate

runners.name: Lord Maher
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far at Market Rasen in July (first-time visor) and he was keeping on in second when brought down at the last here (2m2f, good to soft) in October; heavy defeat when last seen in December but it's still fairly early days and he's not dismissed on this return.
runners.comment: Keeping on when brought down here in October; fairly early days; not dismissed on return

runners.name: All Authorized
runners.spotlight: Course bumper and hurdle winner who won Plumpton handicap hurdle off 18lb higher last April; subsequent starts over fences didn't go well and he resumes chase career having been well held over hurdles on last three starts.
runners.comment: Dangerously handicapped but has been struggling over hurdles and fences for a while

runners.name: Thickthorn Tom
runners.spotlight: Much the best of two chase starts when third of 11 at Exeter (3m, good to soft) in March; drops back in trip with a tongue-tie joining his cheekpieces; unexposed and he's respected for top yard.
runners.comment: Second chase start when third (3m) latest; tongue-tie added and unexposed for top yard

runners.name: Misteroddsocks
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery before he cashed in on a falling mark to open account over C&D (good to soft) last month; up 5lb for that head success but recent form counts for plenty in this race and he's a big player.
runners.comment: Wind surgery before opening account over C&D (good to soft) last month; big player

runners.name: Jaffa Cake
runners.spotlight: Favourite when runner-up on bumper debut but little to shout about over hurdles and beaten a long way on his chase debut over C&D (good) in this headgear combination three weeks ago; respected yard but he needs to show more.
runners.comment: Hurdles form moderate; beaten a long way on C&D chase debut three weeks ago

races.race_name: Southern Cranes And Access Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Hurst Hill
runners.spotlight: Just 1-17 over hurdles, the win gained at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last summer, but she made a satisfactory reappearance last month and her current mark is workable; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Just 1-17 over hurdles but made a satisfactory return last month and this mark is workable

runners.name: Lady Henrietta
runners.spotlight: Absent since struggling to cope with 3m on heavy ground in January but made all under Elliott England at Leicester (2m4f, heavy) in December and was placed on good ground in the autumn; no surprise if she features.
runners.comment: Given break after finding 3m on heavy ground too taxing in January; could have a say

runners.name: Renoir
runners.spotlight: Now 5lb lower than winning winning at Hereford (2m, good) in March 2025 but, following a break, could not justify favouritism when midfield at Stratford (2m2f; good; first run after wind op) last month; sports refitted cheekpieces and tries a new trip here; needs that recent run to have done him good.
runners.comment: Made favourite last month, after a break, but finished in midfield; tries new trip

runners.name: For Old Times Sake
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles, having struggled in his last two handicaps (2m4f/2mf, good; warm favourite on first occasion); goes up in trip fitted with new tongue-tie; has a lot to prove but can't be written off yet.
runners.comment: Struggled in last two handicaps and now 0-6 over hurdles but not written off just yet

runners.name: Nelson Gate
runners.spotlight: Fair on the Flat; probably only sixth best on merit when fourth on handicap debut here (2m3f, good) three weeks ago but positives can be drawn from that performance and he might have more to offer over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Just a respectable fourth here (2m3f) on recent handicap debut but may have more to offer

runners.name: Jager Time
runners.spotlight: Opened account over hurdles in 16-runner handicap at Huntingdon (3m1f, good) in November, his eighth attempt; not seen since and not sure to benefit from this drop back in trip but further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Absent since winning 16-runner Huntingdon handicap in November but may progress again

runners.name: Gone Country
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in first two handicaps (2m, soft/good; raced too freely last time); needs significant improvement but his pedigree and his Irish point form suggest this step up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two 2m handicaps but may fare better over today's longer trip

runners.name: Anothermanicmonday
runners.spotlight: Jumped left throughout and was beaten about 60l on recent handicap debut at Exeter (2m2f, good); this switch to a left-handed track is an obvious move but he needs to take a big step forward over the new trip.
runners.comment: Beaten about 60l on recent handicap debut at Exeter (2m2f); up in trip here

runners.name: Porridge
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles; excelled herself when 19l-fourth in maiden here (2m3f, good to soft) last month, prompting her mark to rise from 51 to 92; now needs to build on that performance on this handicap return.
runners.comment: Excelled herself when fourth in course maiden last month; new mark looks tough, though

runners.name: Tell The Tale
runners.spotlight: Safely held in first two handicaps (2m3f/3m, good to soft) and absent since January but remains unexposed (particularly on good ground) and is worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Safely held in both handicaps (2m3f/3m) but may yet do better; market check advised

runners.name: Counteract
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner for Suzy Smith in 2021; returned from 881-day absence with heavy defeat over hurdles here (3m2f, good) for new stable three weeks ago but ran well to a point and may benefit from this drop back in trip; 2lb wrong but worth a second look.
runners.comment: Ultimately well beaten but ran well to a point here last month, after a long absence

races.race_name: V2 Radio Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Gone In Sixty
runners.spotlight: Sole success came when wide-margin winner over hurdles at Fakenham just under one year ago but he's finished runner-up on 12 occasions, including five times in succession over fences between September and November; had wind surgery after heavy defeat over hurdles at Windsor over five months ago; market might guide on this return.
runners.comment: Five-time runner-up over fences; wind surgery followed heavy hurdles defeat in November

runners.name: Grain Trade
runners.spotlight: Ready win when beating seven others at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) on his second start chasing and looked set to defy a penalty when coming down at the last over C&D (good to soft) in October; jumping has been a bit of an issue since and he was narrowly in front when again coming down at the final fence back at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) one month ago; hood joins his regular tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Jumping has became an issue but he would be a major player with a clear round; hood added

runners.name: Ballywood
runners.spotlight: Down to this mark when nearly winning for the first time in seven years at Huntingdon in March and a slightly lesser effort when third at Hereford (also 2m, good) came just one week later; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Pipped off this mark two runs back; slightly lesser effort came only one week later

runners.name: Holy Legend
runners.spotlight: Just four starts over fences and, after finishing tailed off in the first and refusing in the second, he showed he might be getting the hang of things when sixth of ten at Exeter (2m3f, good; weakened) last month; needs to take another step forward but that's not out of the question.
runners.comment: Showed a bit more last time; needs another step forward but that's not out of the question

runners.name: Saint Polo
runners.spotlight: Went close on handicap debut over hurdles at Sedgefield (2m4f) in March before solid third at Taunton (3m, also good) one month later; drops sharply back in trip with cheekpieces enlisted and he remains unexposed on this introduction to fences.
runners.comment: Unexposed in h'cap hurdles and back down in trip for this chase debut; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Daany
runners.spotlight: Turned his attention to chasing fairly late in life but made a solid start when second of six at Wincanton in April; didn't back that up when never travelling at Hereford (also 2m, good) nine days ago and has a question to answer after that.
runners.comment: Solid second on chase debut but never travelled next time; question to answer after that
runners.owner: Duckhaven D Kenealy W Sprigg M Sprigg

runners.name: Millies Mite
runners.spotlight: Course regular who has won twice (about 2m3f, good) and been placed on another four occasions from 15 visits; last won just over a year ago but looked to have something to offer when unseating four out in April and it was too far out to tell how she'd have fared when falling, again here, last time; 2lb wrong but she's not dismissed.
runners.comment: Course regular; prominent when unseating penultimate start; 2lb wrong but not dismissed

runners.name: Model Approach
runners.spotlight: Beaten a combined 70l in two soft-ground starts over fences but he won his third hurdle race when picking up a 12-runner Ffos Las handicap (2m, good) off this mark nine days ago; 5lb well in on handicap marks if he can transfer that form to chasing and there should be no ground excuses this time.
runners.comment: Beaten combined 70l in two soft-ground chases; 5lb well in after hurdles win (good) latest

races.race_name: Best Of British Events Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Red One
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; closely related to useful winners Guard Your Dreams (2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Carys' Commodity (2m3f-2m6f hurdle/chase); dam unraced half-sister to useful bumper/jumps winner The Jigsaw Man; with a top stable; must be considered.
runners.comment: Represents a top stable and is closely related to two useful jumpers; shortlisted

runners.name: Short Sell
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to stable's smart hurdler Ch'tibello and a smart French Flat racer; bought for £40,000 after comfortably winning a good-ground maiden point in March; ought to make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Bought for £40,000 after comfortably winning a maiden point in April; likely contender

runners.name: My Lady d'Ex
runners.spotlight: Attracted some market support but was last of the nine finishers on debut at Newton Abbot (good) last month; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Attracted some market support but was last of the nine finishers on debut at Newton Abbot

runners.name: Dernier Set
runners.spotlight: £20,000 3yo; tenth foal; half-brother to seven winners including fairly useful hurdler Good Ball; dam placed on Flat, closely related to 10.7f Listed Flat and hurdle winner Great Pretender; stable has 18% strike-rate in bumpers; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Half-brother to seven winners including fairly useful hurdler Good Ball; a possible

runners.name: Horethorne
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected mare but was well held when third of six at Exeter (good) last month and almost certainly needs significant improvement.
runners.comment: Well held when third of six at Exeter last month; improvement probably required

runners.name: Lord Forlife
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to six winners, one useful on the Flat in France and one useful over hurdles there; dam 1m 2yo winner, half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Lady Deauville; stable has fair bumper record; market may guide.
runners.comment: Out of a half-sister to a Group 3 Flat winner; stable has fair bumper record

runners.name: Cameron De Fiacre
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros 2yo; third foal; half-brother to two French jumps winners; dam unraced half-sister to a French hurdle/chase winner; stable's excellent 27% strike-rate in bumpers brings him into the equation.
runners.comment: Stable's excellent 27% strike-rate in bumpers brings him into the equation

runners.name: Murphy d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Out of a French hurdle/chase winner; only fifth of seven in a point bumper last month and probably faces much stronger opposition here.
runners.comment: Out of a French hurdle/chase winner; only fifth of seven in a point bumper last month

runners.name: Mysterland
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros 3yo; third foal; half-brother to fair hurdler Ip Up; dam French hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to high-class cross-country chaser Easysland; stable has occasional bumper winners.
runners.comment: Half-brother to fair hurdler Ip Up; stable has occasional bumper winners

runners.name: Norff N Sauff
runners.spotlight: 24,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; half-brother to fair hurdler/chaser Quick Sharpener and a point winner; dam unraced half-sister to hurdle/chase winner Off The Charts; stable does pretty well in bumpers.
runners.comment: Half-brother to fair hurdler/chaser Quick Sharpener; stable does pretty well in bumpers

runners.name: Roy's Jules
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to stable's smart bumper performer Commanche Red; last of five over C&D (soft) on debut two months ago; today's faster ground needs to show him in a much better light.
runners.comment: Last of five over over C&D (albeit on soft ground) on debut in March
runners.owner: The Les & Jan Gilbert Racing Legacy

runners.name: Meli Melo d'Angos
runners.spotlight: First foal from a French 2m4f/2m5f chase winner; ran well to a point but dropped out rather tamely and was beaten about 24l when eighth of 11 at Hereford (good) last month; needs that run to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Ran well to a point but dropped out rather tamely on debut at Hereford last month

races.race_name: Prix Esoterique (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Chica De Ciudad
runners.owner: Paul De Chevigny & Mme Aoileann Garavaglia-Drion

races.race_name: Prix de l'Avre (Listed Race) (Turf)

runners.name: Montsecret
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Jean-Claude Seroul & G Augustin-Normand

races.race_name: Haras du Lion - Prix Urban Sea (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Le Combat Continue
runners.owner: Mme Genevieve Meunier, Elevage Des Vallons Et Al

races.race_name: Grosser Preis der Sparkasse Dortmund (Listed) (4yo+ Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Gesa
runners.owner: Frau Dr Stephanie Hehlgans & Jochen Nass

races.race_name: Lindum York Handicap

runners.name: Arklow Lad
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won on stable debut at Chelmsford (5f, AW) in February before good third at Wolverhampton (6f, AW), and he was back on the scoresheet in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) last month; could continue to progress for his new connections; chance.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who is 2-3 for his new connections and is in with a chance

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark in small field at Southwell (5f, AW) in March; ran pretty well on next two starts but down the field at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) latest and others are preferred in a hotter race than he's used to.
runners.comment: Won off this mark in small field on AW in March but others preferred in this hot contest

runners.name: Heavenly Heather
runners.spotlight: 5yo who raced over further until this year, and her three career-best RPRs have come on her three attempts at 5f; kept on well for close fourth of 14 at Musselburgh (5f, soft) last month on a rare outing on turf, and she's an each-way contender if handling the forecast better ground.
runners.comment: Has been doing well this year over 5f, at which she is unexposed; each-way contender

runners.name: Air Force One
runners.spotlight: Beat 18 rivals to win over C&D (good) last July and went close over 5.4f here on his next two starts; regained the winning thread at Beverley (5f, good to soft) four weeks ago and he's only 2lb higher today; respected.
runners.comment: Ran some big races here last season and won at Beverley four weeks ago; respected

runners.name: Ziggy's Triton
runners.spotlight: Back on better ground when he returned to form with a win at Musselburgh (5f, good) last month, taking his strike-rate to 5-22; did well to come out of top from a wide draw and this 4yo could go well up 5lb.
runners.comment: Returned to form to win despite wide draw at Musselburgh and he could go well up 5lb

runners.name: Sturlasson
runners.spotlight: Did well for new yard last autumn, winning three times (5f, AW/good to soft), and he returned to form when second of six at Bath (5f, good) recently; however, he may prove vulnerable in this hotter contest.
runners.comment: Returned to form when 2nd of six at Bath but may prove vulnerable in this hotter contest

runners.name: Lethal Nymph
runners.spotlight: Two wins last autumn and he's better than he's shown at Pontefract and Ascot of late; however, this is tougher and he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Two wins last autumn but he needs to better this spring's form, and this race is tough

runners.name: Redorange
runners.spotlight: Last season's 3yo campaign ended with two below-par runs but he may have been ready for a break; reappears on a competitive mark based on last summer's form in big-field 5f handicaps and Ryan Moore, who has an excellent 33% strike-rate for Clive Cox, is booked; could be involved.
runners.comment: Reappears on a competitive mark and Ryan Moore has excellent 33% strike-rate for Clive Cox

runners.name: Blinky
runners.spotlight: Won two 5f AW handicaps early last year and good fourth of 16 in the 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June, when 3lb higher; however, he's 0-11 since his last win and others arrive with more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Won two 5f AW handicaps early last year but 0-11 since; others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Corolla Point
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced, ex-Godolphin 4yo; won on stable debut at Haydock (5f, good) last September and followed up later that month (5f, soft); reappeared with close third to Air Force One at Beverley (5f, good to soft) four weeks ago and this unexposed sort could still have plenty more to offer.
runners.comment: Won first 2 starts for this yard; close 3rd on return; unexposed; could have more to offer

runners.name: Against The Wind
runners.spotlight: Third of 22 in 3yo handicap over C&D (good to firm) at this meeting 12 months ago and progressive on AW since; soft ground may not have suited at Musselburgh last time and he's had wind surgery since; however, a career best is needed today and he has to prove he's just as good on turf as AW.
runners.comment: Has run well in big field over C&D but it's possible he's superior on AW; has had wind op

runners.name: Hammer The Hammer
runners.spotlight: Second of 24 in the 5f 3yo handicap at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm) last June and he showed good speed before fading into sixth of 19 over 6f here (good) last October, when last seen; wind op since; the drop back in trip could be a positive move on his reappearance; can make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Showed good speed over 6f here last October and the return to 5f could be a positive move

runners.name: Copper Knight
runners.spotlight: Hugely admirable 12yo whose seven York wins include this race in 2019 and 2021, and he built upon last month's reappearance second at Ripon (5f, good) to win at Beverley (5f, good to soft) ten days ago; however, there's a suspicion he may find a big-field Class 2 such as this too hot nowadays.
runners.comment: Prolific York winner; scored at Beverley latest; however, this 12yo may find this too hot

runners.name: Eternal Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Trainer won this last year with subsequent Group 1 winner American Affair; progressive last season and she's 1lb lower than when a close third of 18 over C&D (good) last October; however, well beaten on both starts this season (irregular heartbeat last time) and a major turnaround is needed.
runners.comment: Progressive last season and ran really well over C&D last October; yet to shine this term

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: He's been an improved model this year and won a shade cosily at Beverley (5f, good) last month; his subsequent run at Musselburgh (5f, good) three days later, when fourth, may have come too soon and further progress can't be ruled out, but it's necessary with this step up in grade.
runners.comment: Improved this year; further progress not ruled out, but necessary with step up in grade

runners.name: Luna A Inbhir Nis
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was a highly progressive 3yo last season, making it six wins for the campaign when scoring here (5.4f, good) last July; very close third of 19 over this C&D (good to firm) next time; suited by this course and could give a good account on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Front-runner who was a highly progressive 3yo last season, winning here; could go well

runners.name: Toca Madera
runners.spotlight: His form has gone the wrong way since he was third in this last year (good to firm), behind two subsequent Group-race winners, and he needs a good step forward from his recent stable/seasonal debut at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) where he was seventh; however, he's 8lb lower than 12 months ago and could be just the type that David O'Meara excels with; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Third in this last year off 8lb higher; regressive since but early days with his new yard
runners.owner: L Bond & Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi

runners.name: Jakajaro
runners.spotlight: Snapped a lengthy losing sequence when making virtually all to win at Newmarket (5f, good) last month on his reappearance; he needs to back up that performance but did it comfortably last time and, following a 5lb rise, remains well treated on his historic exploits; a possible.
runners.comment: Comfortable win at Newmarket on reappearance; remains well treated on historic exploits

runners.name: Brazen Bolt
runners.spotlight: Won big-field handicaps over C&D (good to firm) and at Goodwood (5f, good) last July, and this 9yo returned from wind surgery with a back-to-form, neck second at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; Harry Vigors removes a valuable 7lb and this prominent-racer could be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last July and back on song when going close on return; could be bang there

runners.name: Stormy Impact
runners.spotlight: Won the big-field 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June and landed another quality handicap at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in July; she lacks a recent run and there is a suspicion that 6f is her optimum trip, but she'll get a strong pace to aim at today and is not ruled out off a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Two wins last season, including the 3yo Dash at Epsom; the strong pace will help today

runners.name: Bergerac
runners.spotlight: 8yo who was inconsistent last season and lacks a recent run; however, he's just 1lb higher than when both second of 13 over C&D (good) last May and winning at Hamilton (5f, good to soft) next time; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: 8yo who was inconsistent last season but is effective here and is not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Squealer
runners.spotlight: Won off 7lb higher over C&D (good) last June and he's one to watch in the betting; an underwhelming autumn was followed by a fairly encouraging reappearance run at Beverley last month, but he was down the field at Thirsk last time and is back to having something to prove.
runners.comment: Won off 7lb higher over C&D last June but his form has gone the wrong way

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gallivanted
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner for this yard; resumed Flat career with close third at Thirsk (1m4f) on handicap debut in this sphere last month and justified strong support when narrowly beating Cruden over this trip at Goodwood (also good) a fortnight ago; unexposed and the best is probably yet to come.
runners.comment: Justified support when narrowly beating Cruden over 1m6f latest; unexposed; key player

runners.name: Just An Hour
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who last scored over 1m2f at Windsor (good to firm) off just 1lb lower in July; runner-up over C&D in October, however, and confirmed this trip holds no fears when second again at Nottingham (also good) 18 days ago; player.
runners.comment: Last won at 1m2f but runner-up over C&D last term and again over 1m6f 18 days ago; player

runners.name: Personal Best
runners.spotlight: Won sole start at two (heavy) and a series of solid efforts from 1m3f to 1m6f last year included win at Ffos Las (1m6f, soft; acts on good) in June; rain would likely increase her chances but still respected on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Solid efforts last year and won (1m6f, soft; acts on good) in June; respected on return
runners.owner: Hot To Trot 1 & Mrs R Pease

runners.name: Tryfan
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner whose sole success on turf came over C&D (good to firm) just under one year ago; also scored off this mark on AW (1m6f) in September but has been very in and out on last four starts, including when tried in a hood (removed) and beaten 32l in valuable AW event last time; drops back down in grade from lower turf mark and can make presence felt if on a going day.
runners.comment: Sole turf win came over C&D (good to firm); inconsistent of late; threat if on a going day

runners.name: Anniversary
runners.spotlight: Won Newmarket maiden on his debut in 2024 but hasn't kicked on from good juvenile form; fair efforts when fifth in quite valuable AW handicaps in February and March but he proved a disappointing favourite on return to turf when beaten 14l at Beverley (also 2m, good to soft) last month; others hold greater appeal.
runners.comment: Fair efforts in quite valuable AW h'caps in February/March but disappointed on turf latest

runners.name: Cruden
runners.spotlight: Won maiden Flat and hurdle race for Jessie Harrington in 2024; big run for this yard when keeping on well from the rear to give Gallivanted a fright at Goodwood (1m6f, good; 25-1) a fortnight ago; had to come down the outer that day and it's no guarantee his old adversary will confirm those placings.
runners.comment: Big run for yard when giving Gallivanted a fright (1m6f, good; 25-1) latest; contender

runners.name: Mr Freedom
runners.spotlight: Six-time Flat winner who scored a fifth win on turf when taking Sandown handicap (1m6f, good) off 2lb lower in July 2024; also a multiple winner over hurdles who was on the premises when taking heavy last-flight fall at Plumpton just over one year ago; interesting to see how he goes in the market ahead of this comeback.
runners.comment: Won (1m6f) off 2lb lower in July 2024; heavy hurdle fall when last seen; market helpful

runners.name: Individualiste
runners.spotlight: Winner over hurdles for Paul Nicholls and fences for Gavin Cromwell but pulled up at big prices in both hurdle starts for this yard; visor deployed on his belated handicap debut in this sphere.
runners.comment: Pulled up both h'dle starts for this yard; visor on for belated h'cap debut in this sphere

runners.name: Alfred Boucher
runners.spotlight: Won sole visit here (1m handicap in 2019); in the care of Ian Williams when landing 2m York handicap in August 2022 and ran a mighty race when pipped in the Ebor a fortnight later; pulled up at Chester the following month and seen just three times since, including when down the field in two starts for this yard last year; drops in grade from falling mark; watch the market on return.
runners.comment: Lightly raced since 2022 but on a dangerous mark; watch the market on this return

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Hambleton Handicap

runners.name: Sea Force
runners.spotlight: Won at Yarmouth (1m1f, soft) last September on final 3yo start and returned to form when third to Mirsky in the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago in reapplied cheekpieces (retained), which he may well have won granted a clear run; lightly raced and in top hands; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: May well have won the Thirsk Hunt Cup granted a clear run and he's just 1lb higher today

runners.name: First Principle
runners.spotlight: Having been gelded last autumn he enjoyed a good stint on AW, winning twice (1m/8.6f); soundly beaten at Newcastle (1m, AW) last time and he needs to transfer his improvement back to turf, but he remains lightly raced for his top yard and is not discounted under Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: AW improvement stalled last time but he remains lightly raced and is not discounted

runners.name: Walsingham
runners.spotlight: Ran just four times last year, winning on three occasions (1m, good/soft), and back on the scoresheet at Pontefract (1m, good) a fortnight ago on his second start of the season; did it cosily last time and a 4lb rise may not stop this thriving 6yo.
runners.comment: Did it cosily at Pontefract last time and 4lb rise may not stop this thriving 6yo

runners.name: Shout
runners.spotlight: Progressive 3yo last season (on various ground), winning a big-field 1m handicap at Ascot in September; reappeared with encouraging run in the Lincoln at Doncaster and he's not written off, but he was somewhat underwhelming in the Spring Cup at Newbury last time when favourite.
runners.comment: Progressive 3yo last season; not written off but underwhelming in the Spring Cup last time

runners.name: Cerulean Bay
runners.spotlight: Progressive last term, winning two in a row at Goodwood (1m, good to soft/good) in the autumn, and kept on well for a close fourth on last month's reappearance at Haydock (7f, good to firm); solid claims back up to 1m.
runners.comment: Progressive last term and kept on well for close 4th over 7f on reappearance; solid claims

runners.name: Old Cock
runners.spotlight: Won this off 1lb lower last year (good to firm); however, soundly beaten over C&D (good to firm) last August on final run for Ed Bethell and down the field on last month's stable/seasonal debut at Newbury (1m, good).
runners.comment: Won this off 1lb lower last year but arrives with something to prove this time around

runners.name: Eldrickjones
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last year's best form (including on turf) and ran okay when third of six at Southwell (1m, AW) last month; however, he's been hard to win with on grass and looks vulnerable back up in grade.
runners.comment: Ran okay when third of six at Southwell last month but looks vulnerable back up in grade

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has returned in top form this term, finishing third at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) then winning the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago; effective over C&D; could be bang there once more up 3lb for last time.
runners.comment: Won the Thirsk Hunt Cup 12 days ago and could be bang there once more up 3lb

runners.name: Duke's Command
runners.spotlight: Two 1m wins on turf last season and added another on AW at Lingfield (1m2f) in December; however, he needs to produce a bigger performance than he's managed across his four outings this year.
runners.comment: Three wins in 2025 but has to produce a bigger run than he's managed so far this year

runners.name: Point Of Contact
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts last July-September after moving into handicaps, the best run coming over C&D (good to firm) in August when close fifth of 17; gelded since last seen and it's possible this lightly raced 4yo will have more to offer for his good yard this term.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who has been gelded and may have more to offer for his good yard

runners.name: Diego Ventura
runners.spotlight: 7f Listed winner at Longchamp last May and he ran some good races subsequently in Group/Listed contests; below par on last month's reappearance at Longchamp but may have needed the run; could go well in first handicap.
runners.comment: Solid performer in Group/Listed races last season; could go well in his first handicap

runners.name: Maybe Not
runners.spotlight: Three wins last June-September (7f-1m) along with 1l second of 18 in a fillies' handicap here (7f, good to firm), and she's better than she showed at Newmarket (1m, good) on her final outing last season; this lightly raced 4yo is in excellent hands to continue to progress and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Three wins last June-September along with a big run here; she's one to consider

runners.name: Galeron
runners.spotlight: On a competitive mark on last year's best efforts but he's without a win since September 2022 and finished merely in mid-division on reappearance in the Lincoln at Doncaster in March (unseated rider on the way to post).
runners.comment: On a competitive mark on last year's best efforts but merely mid-division in the Lincoln

runners.name: Thunder Roar
runners.spotlight: 7yo who was better than ever last season, a campaign that concluded with a fine second of 17 over C&D (good); races off the same mark today but he hasn't shone on his two outings this year.
runners.comment: Fine second off this mark over C&D last October but hasn't shone on either run this year

runners.name: Theoryofeverything
runners.spotlight: Second of 14 off 1lb higher behind Thunder Roar at Ayr (1m) last September on his favoured soft ground, and didn't run badly when 4l 11th of 21 in the Lincoln at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) on his reappearance; however, others have more compelling credentials.
runners.comment: Best form has come on soft ground; midfield in Lincoln on reappearance; others appeal more

runners.name: Croupier
runners.spotlight: Won this for the Crisfords in 2023 when 1lb higher; missed all of 2024 and 2025 and down the field at 40-1 on last month's stable debut at Haydock; he may have needed that run but has to prove how much ability he retains.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023; down the field at Haydock last month when back from very long absence

runners.name: Godwinson
runners.spotlight: Won the Lincoln at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last March and followed up at Newcastle (1m, AW); of interest on those efforts but he went off the boil on his final three starts for William Haggas; needs to get back on track on stable debut (remains in same ownership); slow ground would aid cause.
runners.comment: Last year's Lincoln winner but went off the boil later in the season; has changed yards

runners.name: Point Lynas
runners.spotlight: Below best on final two starts last season and same story on sole run this term, in Qatar in February; also has a hefty mark to defy but this front-runner was a fine second under an enterprising ride in a Group 2 at Ascot last July and has a strong record here, finishing second in this race in 2023 and winning it in 2024; capable of very bold bid from the inside stall.
runners.comment: Strong record at York, including in this race; capable of very bold bid from inside stall
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Vafortino
runners.spotlight: 8yo who is without a win since December 2023 but he showed spark in Dubai this winter and ran big races on his two previous visits to York; returns to a handy on a fair mark and could have an each-way shout.
runners.comment: 8yo who is without a win since December but is worth a second look in view of York form
runners.owner: D MacAuliffe, Anoj Don & Dahab Racing

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: See The Fire
runners.spotlight: Pulverised her seven rivals in this on her second outing of last season, scoring by a whopping 12l on good to firm; couldn't quite reproduce that high-class RPR at Group 1 level in five subsequent outings in 2025 but mostly ran well, including a close second in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, soft); didn't have much to spare at the line when 1-3 for a 1m2f Nottingham Listed race on reappearance but cruised through most of that contest and will likely be a lot closer to her best this time; again the one to beat in this.
runners.comment: Romped home in this last year and the one to beat again on the back of reappearance win

runners.name: Diamond Rain
runners.spotlight: An improved performer last year, winning a good-ground Listed race at Haydock (reappearance) and AW Group 3 at Newcastle around 1m2f before progressing again to place in Grade 1s on firm at Woodbine (1m2f) and Del Mar (1m3f; Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Turf); bang there on form but the fact her top stable has gone nearly three weeks without a winner has to be a slight concern.
runners.comment: Good form shout and capable fresh but top yard quiet in recent weeks

runners.name: Red Letter
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Listed race at Killarney (1m, soft) and Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f, good to yielding) last summer; creditable third of 12 in 1m2f Group 3 back at the Curragh (soft) on reappearance 25 days ago; likely to give a good account but has a bit to find on ratings.
runners.comment: Listed/Group 3 wins last year; solid reappearance; should run well but bit to find on form

runners.name: Fallen Angel
runners.spotlight: Won three 1m Group 1s against her own sex last year, namely the Prix Rothschild, Matron and Sun Chariot (all good/good to yielding); tackles 1m2f for just the second time on her seasonal return (wasn't strong at the finish when fourth in the 2024 Prix de l'Opera on previous attempt but it was only just short of her best RPR at the time); has to carry a 3lb penalty and wasn't at her best on her reappearance in 2024 or 2025; top mare but there are reasons to oppose her this time.
runners.comment: Top mare but the trip, an absence and 3lb penalty are possible reasons to oppose her

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Action
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Westow Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Aspect Island
runners.spotlight: Won Yarmouth nursery (6f, soft) in September but better form when third in Group 3 Cornwallis at Newmarket (good) and Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint (again 5f, firm) the following month; looked to have trained on when beaten around 2l in all-aged Group 3 at Newmarket four weeks ago; holds Group 1 entries over 5f and 6f at Royal Ascot and now drops back into his own age group; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Third in G3 and Grade 1 (again 5f) last term; solid return last month; sets the standard

runners.name: Simplify
runners.spotlight: Improving filly who made all in Thirsk novice (6f, good) on her final start of last term and carried on the good work when making a winning seasonal/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in March; completed the hat-trick with best effort yet in Thirsk handicap (5f, good) last month and she's not discounted in her bid for the four-timer representing last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Improving filly who's not discounted in bid for four-timer from last year's winning yard

runners.name: Revival Power
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last year, making all in Listed event over C&D (good to firm) at the Ebor meeting in August and Doncaster's Group 2 Flying Childers (5f, good) three weeks later; holds a Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot and her sister Winter Power, also a Group-race winning juvenile, won this race in 2021 before landing the Nunthorpe later that year; carries a penalty on this return but a bold bid looks likely.
runners.comment: Won C&D Listed and G2 (5f, good) last term; holds G1 entry; penalised but a big player

runners.name: Wor Faayth
runners.spotlight: Made all in Hamilton maiden (5f, good) in June and won French Listed race (6f, good) on her next start in July; little went right in the Group 2 Lowther here (good to firm) the following month and wasn't disgraced in Chantilly Group 3 (6f, good to soft) when last seen in September; unexposed at this trip and she's not without interest ahead of her comeback.
runners.comment: 5f winner who won French Listed race (6f, good) in July; not without interest on return

runners.name: Dickensian
runners.spotlight: All starts at 5f on good or good to firm; no wins since his debut success at Musselburgh 13 months ago but he ran a belter when second of 20 in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot; again very useful form when beaten around 2l by Revival Power in Listed C&D event in August and Doncaster Group 2 three weeks later; solid each-way claims on this reappearance.
runners.comment: No win since debut but very useful form when 3rd to Revival Power in Listed/G2; e-w claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Boston Dan
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in four-runner Hamilton maiden (5f, good to soft) but it was a fine effort when second at 66-1 in very valuable sales race at the Ebor meeting (6f, good to firm) and bettered that form when runner-up in Listed event back here (good) in October; floundered on heavy ground next time, however, and down the field in Newmarket handicap on return; work to do even if this track brings out the best in him again.
runners.comment: Has gone well here but work to do even if this track brings out the best in him again

runners.name: Manatee Mehmas
runners.spotlight: 105,000gns yearling whose dam is closely related to 6f Group 2 winner Tasleet; sent off odds-on for his debut in Doncaster novice (5f, good to firm) where he made stylish headway before dispatching his main market rival with ease 19 days ago; goes straight into Listed company and he's a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Sparkling debut in 5f novice (odds-on) 19 days ago; fascinating prospect up in grade

runners.name: Black Star Boy
runners.spotlight: Newbury maiden (good to soft) winner who was gelded after finishing down the field in Listed event here (also 6f) in October; coped well with the drop to 5f when taking good step forward to land Sandown handicap (good) on his reappearance three weeks ago; unexposed and likely capable of better again.
runners.comment: Good step forward on first go at 5f with h'cap win on return; unexposed and can do better

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: Won Beverley maiden (soft; debut) and Chester nursery (good to soft) last term; again scored on first start of the year when taking AW handicap (also 5f) in March; possibly found 6f a stretch again when disappointing at Chester last Wednesday but this looks too warm even if she does get back to her best.
runners.comment: Found 6f a stretch when disappointing at Chester last Wednesday but this looks too warm

runners.name: Palmeira
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but she went close when beaten a neck in Newbury Listed race in August; similar form when seventh to Revival Power in Doncaster Group 2 in September and when sixth of 14 in Bath Listed event (also 5f, good) on last month's return; likely to find a few too good again.
runners.comment: Useful filly but she's 0-11 and likely to find a few too good again

runners.name: Exclamation
runners.spotlight: Raced mainly at 5f; won Doncaster novice (good to soft) last summer and bettered that form when fifth to Revival Power in Doncaster Group 2 (80-1) in September; doubled his tally when producing career best to make all in Chester handicap (good) on Friday; drawn on the wing but could be the sort to run above himself.
runners.comment: Made all in Chester handicap (5f, good) on Friday; could be the sort to run above himself

races.race_name: Grosser Preis der Sparkasse Dortmund (Listed) (4yo+ Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Gesa
runners.owner: Frau Dr Stephanie Hehlgans & Jochen Nass

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: See The Fire
runners.spotlight: Pulverised her seven rivals in this on her second outing of last season, scoring by a whopping 12l on good to firm; couldn't quite reproduce that high-class RPR at Group 1 level in five subsequent outings in 2025 but mostly ran well, including a close second in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, soft); didn't have much to spare at the line when 1-3 for a 1m2f Nottingham Listed race on reappearance but cruised through most of that contest and will likely be a lot closer to her best this time; again the one to beat in this.
runners.comment: Romped home in this last year and the one to beat again on the back of reappearance win

runners.name: Diamond Rain
runners.spotlight: An improved performer last year, winning a good-ground Listed race at Haydock (reappearance) and AW Group 3 at Newcastle around 1m2f before progressing again to place in Grade 1s on firm at Woodbine (1m2f) and Del Mar (1m3f; Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Turf); bang there on form but the fact her top stable has gone nearly three weeks without a winner has to be a slight concern.
runners.comment: Good form shout and capable fresh but top yard quiet in recent weeks

runners.name: Red Letter
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Listed race at Killarney (1m, soft) and Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f, good to yielding) last summer; creditable third of 12 in 1m2f Group 3 back at the Curragh (soft) on reappearance 25 days ago; likely to give a good account but has a bit to find on ratings.
runners.comment: Listed/Group 3 wins last year; solid reappearance; should run well but bit to find on form

runners.name: Fallen Angel
runners.spotlight: Won three 1m Group 1s against her own sex last year, namely the Prix Rothschild, Matron and Sun Chariot (all good/good to yielding); tackles 1m2f for just the second time on her seasonal return (wasn't strong at the finish when fourth in the 2024 Prix de l'Opera on previous attempt but it was only just short of her best RPR at the time); has to carry a 3lb penalty and wasn't at her best on her reappearance in 2024 or 2025; top mare but there are reasons to oppose her this time.
runners.comment: Top mare but the trip, an absence and 3lb penalty are possible reasons to oppose her

races.race_name: Prix Esoterique (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Chica De Ciudad
runners.owner: Paul De Chevigny & Mme Aoileann Garavaglia-Drion

races.race_name: Haras du Lion - Prix Urban Sea (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Le Combat Continue
runners.owner: Mme Genevieve Meunier, Elevage Des Vallons Et Al

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Action
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Westow Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Aspect Island
runners.spotlight: Won Yarmouth nursery (6f, soft) in September but better form when third in Group 3 Cornwallis at Newmarket (good) and Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint (again 5f, firm) the following month; looked to have trained on when beaten around 2l in all-aged Group 3 at Newmarket four weeks ago; holds Group 1 entries over 5f and 6f at Royal Ascot and now drops back into his own age group; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Third in G3 and Grade 1 (again 5f) last term; solid return last month; sets the standard

runners.name: Simplify
runners.spotlight: Improving filly who made all in Thirsk novice (6f, good) on her final start of last term and carried on the good work when making a winning seasonal/handicap debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in March; completed the hat-trick with best effort yet in Thirsk handicap (5f, good) last month and she's not discounted in her bid for the four-timer representing last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Improving filly who's not discounted in bid for four-timer from last year's winning yard

runners.name: Revival Power
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last year, making all in Listed event over C&D (good to firm) at the Ebor meeting in August and Doncaster's Group 2 Flying Childers (5f, good) three weeks later; holds a Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot and her sister Winter Power, also a Group-race winning juvenile, won this race in 2021 before landing the Nunthorpe later that year; carries a penalty on this return but a bold bid looks likely.
runners.comment: Won C&D Listed and G2 (5f, good) last term; holds G1 entry; penalised but a big player

runners.name: Wor Faayth
runners.spotlight: Made all in Hamilton maiden (5f, good) in June and won French Listed race (6f, good) on her next start in July; little went right in the Group 2 Lowther here (good to firm) the following month and wasn't disgraced in Chantilly Group 3 (6f, good to soft) when last seen in September; unexposed at this trip and she's not without interest ahead of her comeback.
runners.comment: 5f winner who won French Listed race (6f, good) in July; not without interest on return

runners.name: Dickensian
runners.spotlight: All starts at 5f on good or good to firm; no wins since his debut success at Musselburgh 13 months ago but he ran a belter when second of 20 in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot; again very useful form when beaten around 2l by Revival Power in Listed C&D event in August and Doncaster Group 2 three weeks later; solid each-way claims on this reappearance.
runners.comment: No win since debut but very useful form when 3rd to Revival Power in Listed/G2; e-w claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Boston Dan
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in four-runner Hamilton maiden (5f, good to soft) but it was a fine effort when second at 66-1 in very valuable sales race at the Ebor meeting (6f, good to firm) and bettered that form when runner-up in Listed event back here (good) in October; floundered on heavy ground next time, however, and down the field in Newmarket handicap on return; work to do even if this track brings out the best in him again.
runners.comment: Has gone well here but work to do even if this track brings out the best in him again

runners.name: Manatee Mehmas
runners.spotlight: 105,000gns yearling whose dam is closely related to 6f Group 2 winner Tasleet; sent off odds-on for his debut in Doncaster novice (5f, good to firm) where he made stylish headway before dispatching his main market rival with ease 19 days ago; goes straight into Listed company and he's a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Sparkling debut in 5f novice (odds-on) 19 days ago; fascinating prospect up in grade

runners.name: Black Star Boy
runners.spotlight: Newbury maiden (good to soft) winner who was gelded after finishing down the field in Listed event here (also 6f) in October; coped well with the drop to 5f when taking good step forward to land Sandown handicap (good) on his reappearance three weeks ago; unexposed and likely capable of better again.
runners.comment: Good step forward on first go at 5f with h'cap win on return; unexposed and can do better

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: Won Beverley maiden (soft; debut) and Chester nursery (good to soft) last term; again scored on first start of the year when taking AW handicap (also 5f) in March; possibly found 6f a stretch again when disappointing at Chester last Wednesday but this looks too warm even if she does get back to her best.
runners.comment: Found 6f a stretch when disappointing at Chester last Wednesday but this looks too warm

runners.name: Palmeira
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but she went close when beaten a neck in Newbury Listed race in August; similar form when seventh to Revival Power in Doncaster Group 2 in September and when sixth of 14 in Bath Listed event (also 5f, good) on last month's return; likely to find a few too good again.
runners.comment: Useful filly but she's 0-11 and likely to find a few too good again

runners.name: Exclamation
runners.spotlight: Raced mainly at 5f; won Doncaster novice (good to soft) last summer and bettered that form when fifth to Revival Power in Doncaster Group 2 (80-1) in September; doubled his tally when producing career best to make all in Chester handicap (good) on Friday; drawn on the wing but could be the sort to run above himself.
runners.comment: Made all in Chester handicap (5f, good) on Friday; could be the sort to run above himself
